review_text:
Our family decided to withdraw our child from Mulberry Preschool Bukit Batok after careful consideration of our experience with the centre, forfeiting our deposit.

Throughout our child’s time there, we had concerns regarding communication about matters affecting our child's emotional well-being. While the teachers and management did meet with us to discuss their observations, we felt that some significant incidents and concerns could have been communicated to us earlier so that we could work together more effectively to support her.

To be fair, the centre had previously raised concerns about our child's emotional regulation, which we took seriously by seeking professional assessment and providing support at home. We appreciated the feedback provided and the efforts made by the teachers to manage the needs.

However, as parents, we felt that our expectations regarding communication, transparency, and partnership between home and school were not fully met. Ultimately, we decided that a different preschool environment would be a better fit for our child.

Since moving to a new preschool, our child has settled in well and has been happy in the new environment.

Every child and family will have a different experience, but we hope this feedback is helpful for parents who value regular communication and close collaboration with their child's preschool. We would encourage parents to do their due diligence.

Never regret to choose this school. I had send my girl to school at 18 months old. The transition for my girl to pre-nursery is quite smooth.  The teachers are very loving to the children and I love the weekly updates and photos of our kid’s progress.

Thank you Mulberry Bukit Batok 🙏🏻

​Highly Recommend! Responsible and Dedicated Staff!

Mulberry Bukit Batok is simply amazing. The teachers and staff are incredibly committed and responsible, which gives us so much peace of mind. But more than that, they make school an absolute joy for the children!
​They constantly arrange exciting events and celebrations that make amazing memories. We were blown away by the Mid-Autumn Festival event—a catered buffet dinner, lantern walk around the estate, making play-doh mooncakes, and fun riddles. Plus, their Family Day events are a wonderful way for parents to get involved and have fun with the kids. My child absolutely loves this school! You won't regret sending your child here!

The teachers clearly put in a lot of effort into planning and organizing the Mid-Autumn Festival event. It was well thought out and engaging, and the children had a wonderful time. It was heartwarming to see them so involved and enjoying the activities. Thank you for creating such a meaningful and joyful experience for the kids!

Teacher Jiaqi, Zhao Laoshi, and Jiawen Laoshi are still fondly remembered by my daughter, even though she's no longer at Mulberry. It goes to show how dedicated the team of teachers are in nurturing kids. Highly recommended!

Our son has been with MapleBear Bukit Batok since infant care, and it’s been a really reassuring experience for us as parents.

The teachers and staff have consistently shown a lot of warmth and attentiveness, and it’s clear they genuinely care about the children. We’ve seen how they support and encourage each child’s development, even in the small day-to-day progress.

We value how communicative the team is. The regular updates about his day and curriculum help us feel connected and at ease while he’s there.

Overall, it’s a nurturing and positive environment, and we’re grateful to have Maple Bear Bukit Batok where our son feels comfortable and comes home happy every day!

## Review for MapleBear
**Rating: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐**
We currently have both of our children enrolled here, and it has been a fantastic experience for our family. The staff and teachers are incredibly approachable, always making time for a quick chat and ensuring we feel comfortable and informed.
One of the things we appreciate most is the weekly updates we receive through the school app. It’s great to see photos and read about what the kids have been up to, and it makes following their development very easy. The environment is nurturing, and both our toddler and local preschooler have settled in very well. Highly recommended for parents looking for a school with a dedicated team and great communication!

I’ve a really positive experience with MapleBear Infant @ BB. The teachers are very attentive and caring, and you can tell they are genuine.
I really appreciate the prompt updates throughout the day. Whether it’s about feeding, naps, or just little moments, it helps me stay connected and reassured even when I’m at work. The communication is clear and consistent, which makes a big difference.
Most importantly, I have peace of mind knowing my child is in good hands.

My son has been studying at MapleBear Bukit Batok since he was 2 years old, and we are very happy with the experience. Now at 4, he is always happy to go to school every day without any need to force him, which really shows how comfortable and secure he feels there.

The school offers great enrichment classes, especially in music and mathematics, which have helped build his interest and confidence over the years. The teachers are caring, patient, and dedicated, creating a positive and supportive learning environment.

Our son joined MapleBear Bukit Batok in Nursery after we relocated homes and has since progressed through Junior Kindergarten and now Senior Kindergarten. Having experienced another preschool prior to this, we had clear expectations — structured curriculum, strong communication, and visible developmental outcomes. MapleBear has met, and in several areas exceeded, those expectations.

The school leadership and administrative team run a tight and professional operation. Communication is consistent and proactive, not only when issues arise but as part of an ongoing partnership. What we appreciate most is the quality of teacher observations — specific, nuanced insights into our son’s personality, strengths, and growth areas. The feedback has been practical and actionable, allowing us to reinforce learning meaningfully at home.

Education here extends to parents in a structured way through curriculum briefings, and informally through thoughtful daily interactions. We have also received substantive guidance on extracurricular direction — helping us better understand our son’s emerging interests and temperament rather than simply filling his schedule.

Most importantly, we have seen tangible outcomes.

Our son went from resisting school in his previous environment to genuinely looking forward to attending each day. He talks about what he learns, repeats concepts at home, and shows increased confidence in expressing himself.

Beyond academics, we’ve observed strong character development — not only in our child but among his peers. The children demonstrate empathy, leadership in social settings, and a willingness to help one another. That speaks to intentional culture-building, not just curriculum delivery.

No school is perfect, but for parents who value structured learning, consistent standards, and genuine collaboration between school and home, MapleBear Bukit Batok provides a solid and dependable foundation.

We are grateful for the professionalism of the team and the steady developmental progress we’ve seen over the last four wonderful years with Ms Shally, Gao Laoshi, Ms Wenna, Ms June, Ms Dora and many more!

We have enrolled our child. It’s been 1.5 years, she is happy and learning and enjoying her field trips.
The teachers make sure the child is safe and developing in all aspects and help parents to the growth of child.
The teachers are really kind and friendly and my child improve alot when speaking and learning new knowledge.
Thank you to all teachers of LGH Bukit Batok.

I am very grateful to the teachers and staff in LGH bukit batok for making my son's early learning a positive and happy one. The environment of the school is really good with lots of learning space and clean. Highly recommend!

Went to check out a lot of places for Hadi's school and the moment I stepped into LGH Bukit Batok, I immediately loved it. The centre is clean, air-conditioned, and looks like a super comfortable school for my son to play and learn. Teachers are SUPER friendly! I dont have to worry at all when my son is with them. My son is a very quiet boy in school but at home, I was shocked that he could sing and talk! But his teachers said they couldn't get any word from him haha! He's shy but he has improved a lot. At school, he ate a lot too, good food they have! I will miss LGH so so so much, so does Hadi... They gave us a great experience. Wish Hadi could stay longer in LGH but we are going to stay further from the school.. Thank you so much for the service LGH.
Wonderful nursery with caring teachers and a safe, happy environment. My child has grown so much in confidence and loves going to school every day. Highly recommend！

I’m extremely thankful to all the staff at the Bukit Batok branch of Little Green House. My child has been with them from PG to K2. Even when he has done something wrongly, his teachers are nurturing and guides him to do the correct thing. When my kids are sick, the teachers will check on how they are doing and send their well-wishes. Not just the teachers, the support staff are great too! My kids will come home and sing praises of auntie’s cooking. If we bump into the aunties when I fetch my kids, they are extremely friendly! I am glad to have placed my kids in LGH where I know there is a village to support and nurture and love my kids. Thank you to everyone at LGH!

​Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) offers a highly practical and industry-focused education. The curriculum is regularly updated with input from industry partners, and lecturers, often with professional experience, provide a realistic and engaging learning environment. The focus on project-based learning encourages critical thinking and problem-solving, which I found to be a more effective method of learning than traditional memorization.

​While the polytechnic's modular system can be demanding, with a constant cycle of assignments and projects, it effectively prepares students for the pressures of both higher education and the workplace. The campus life is equally vibrant, with a wide array of CCAs, excellent facilities, and diverse food options.

​Overall, NP provides a well-rounded experience, blending academic rigor with a rich student life. It's a great choice for students who thrive in a hands-on environment and are seeking to build a strong foundation for their future careers.
such a nice campus with best view and good food although quite hilly and requires extra effort to climb hehehe

It sounds like you're planning a trip to Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) Block 23! Here's a quick rundown to help you get there:

1. Take the MRT to Clementi Station.
2. At Clementi Station, exit towards Bus Interchange.
3. Board Bus 154 from Clementi Bus Interchange.
4. Get off at Ngee Ann Polytechnic (NP) stop.

You can use Google Maps or a transit app like Grab or Moovit for the most up-to-date bus schedules and routes. Have a smooth ride to your company event! 🚍🎉..

This is my first time to Bukit Batok polyclinic.  The experience was lousy. The waiting time was long despite I had an appointment. I waited for more than 1.5 hrs for my turn. The staff nurse taking bood test had no experience, she poked twice on my hand and was unable to get the blood flow into the tube and declared that I had "no blood". I requested for another staff to draw my blood, who effectlessly draw out 2 tubes of blood from my hand. The appointments machines were not working.  The payments machines were all down. If our government encourages the people to go digital, then the equipments and support must be good.  How could it happen when all the machines were faulty and no manpower was despatched to salvage the situation? If we were not fully ready, why not have partial of the services digitalise and partial manually? Singapore being coined as one of the top notch medical service provider in the world, and is well known for our efficiency,  I strongly feel that NUHS really has a lot to catch up in improving the services and experiences of the patients.

We would like to formally express our heartfelt gratitude to Teacher Michelle at Genie Hub @ Bukit Batok. It is a small yet cosy centre with an excellent teacher-to-student ratio, which allowed Teacher Michelle to give her students the individual attention they needed to ask questions, clarify doubts, and actively exchange ideas.

Over the past one and a half years, her well-structured weekly notes, engaging lessons, and intensive revision classes prior to the O-Level Physics examinations greatly boosted my child’s confidence in handling Pure Physics. We are incredibly proud that he achieved an A1 for this subject, especially since Physics was his weakest among the three sciences he took.

We would also like to thank Teacher Michelle for her kindness and genuine care. On days when my child was low on energy, she even offered him a hard-boiled egg that she had brought for herself. It is these small yet meaningful acts of kindness that truly motivate and inspire her students to do better in the subject.

Highly recommended to anyone looking for a dedicated, experienced and caring teacher to help with their sciences!

great experience learning at this tuition centre. teacher michelle was patient, made lessons fun and engaging, and helped my grades improve to an A1 as a Sec 4 student. thank you for the bubble tea and the eoy incentive! i really enjoy coming to this tuition, definitely recommend.

My teacher, Jason, is especially good at turning difficult concepts into simple, relatable scenarios, making them much easier to understand and remember. His teaching style is also very flexible and changes according to what students need, whether it’s strengthening basics or focusing on exam techniques.

With his guidance, I was able to improve significantly in just a few months, which I honestly didn’t think was possible before joining. This centre really helped me build confidence in math, and I would highly recommend it to students who struggle or feel stressed about the subject.

Brand new and definitely worth a visit for library lovers, those seeking some peace and quiet, and anyone interested in the history of Bukit Batok, also known as the "Coughing Hill".
Besides offering plenty of seats and reading areas, the library also features interesting sections such as SingLand Soundscape and Time Caverns.
History enthusiasts will appreciate the section showcasing the Former
Ford Factory, the very place where the British Army surrendered to the Japanese Army during World War II.
A modern and thoughtfully designed library that combines learning, history, and tranquillity, making it a wonderful place to spend a few relaxing hours.

The Bukit Batok Public Library has reopened after renovation and now features a refreshed, modern layout. The space feels brighter and more spacious, with well-organised book sections and comfortable reading areas.
There is a good collection of books across different categories, including children’s books, fiction, and non-fiction. The environment is quiet and suitable for reading, studying, or spending some relaxed time with a book.
A great community library in the Bukit Batok area and a nice place to visit if you enjoy reading or need a calm study environment. 📚

We came across the newly reopened public library (after a renovation) located at West Mall in Bukit Batok.

The library's collection of physical books is at the 3rd floor, but it also boasts large interactive displays for readers to check out ebook recommedations in travel, health and adult fiction genres, and to borrow them on the NLB app.

One can access the library from an escalator on the first floor (there is also an entrance and exit on the 3rd floor) and an ambient sound track of birds, rustling leaves and chatter follows the visitor to the 2nd floor. Access the library proper with your SingPass (Singapore citizen).

The 2nd floor has sound installations at various sections like 'Sound Cave' and 'Time Caverns' ( which offer a history of the Bukit Batok area as well as some significant moments of Singapore's history). There is also a cosy area for visitors to read their books.

The library was quite quiet while we were there, though there was quite a number of visitors and readers. This is quite a pleasant surprise to see how our public libraries have evolved. Make a date for a visit to it, if you haven't.

Date of visit : May 2026

If one were to embark upon an intellectual pilgrimage through the hallowed terrain of Singapore’s urban tapestry, few locales induce as much bewildered reverence as the legendary Bukit Gombak—a district whose name alone evokes both mystique and the faint sound of an uncle coughing in the distance.

From the moment one steps foot upon this storied soil, an overwhelming sense of “Ah… so this is it ah?” floods the senses. It is a place where the ordinary becomes extraordinary, not by divine intent, but by sheer, stubborn idiosyncrasy.

---

The Meteorological Madness

The climate of Bukit Gombak deserves its own National Day Parade segment. The heat here is no mere warmth—it is an omnidirectional thermodynamic assault. One does not simply sweat; one exudes moisture like a malfunctioning humidifier undergoing a midlife crisis.

Stand still for five minutes and you will experience all three stages of evaporation, condensation, and emotional breakdown.

---

The Vehicular Symphony

The roads of Bukit Gombak host an eclectic assemblage of vehicles, each contributing to a soundscape best described as “orchestra of slightly frustrated car owners.”

Between the roaring motorcycles, honking cars, and the occasional auntie yelling “OI LOOK OUT LAH”, one cannot help but marvel at the cacophony. It is as though every driver is participating in an unspoken competition for the prestigious Loudest Possible Engine at 8am award.

---

The Architectural Aesthetic

The HDB blocks tower with a quiet dignity, each painted in a shade that can only be described as “existential beige.” They gaze upon you as if whispering,
“Yes… we have seen things. Mostly people doing their laundry.”

The nearby factories complete the picture, standing proudly like metallic monoliths of productivity and mild respiratory discomfort.

---

Recreational Offerings: A Study in Contrast

The iconic Little Guilin sits resplendent, a serene oasis surrounded by lush greenery and calm waters—a tranquil marvel that inspires peace, reflection, and the occasional influencer photoshoot featuring suspiciously dramatic poses.

But venture just thirty seconds away and you are greeted with the unmistakable scent of industrial enthusiasm, reminding you that serenity is but a mere visitor here, not a permanent resident.

Final Verdict

Bukit Gombak is a paradoxical marvel—a tapestry woven with threads of heat, honking, and heartfelt community spirit. It is  chaotic and terrible, filled with anger and gloom.

Would recommend:
✔ For anthropologists
✔ For people who enjoy sweating
✔ For those craving mild adventure without leaving the MRT line

Would not recommend:
✘ For vampires (too sunny)
✘ For anyone allergic to uncles doing tai chi at improbable hours

LONG LIVE CCK!
Bukit Gombak MRT station is NS3 on the North South (Red) line. The station has quite a few shops like Pizza Hut, Mosburger express, 7 Eleven and several ATM’s

Bukit Gombak MRT station is not a big station but it has plenty of things to eat; it even has a mini MOS Burger Express at the station.
The surrounding HDB area also has coffeeshops with plenty of good local food; including Halal food stalls and a few vegan/ vegetarian stalls.
The nearby Zu-Lin Temple Association (竹林寺) has daily free vegan meals for all.

After enjoying the delicious local food in the neighbourhood, one can also visit the Bukit Batok Nature Park, Little Guilin and Bukit Gombak Stadium near the station.
