Without any apologies to the 15 to 20 passengers, she did not even know what she had done wrong. It would be great if the Singapore management could rectify the situation and prevent it from happening again in 2022

This bus interchange is connected to the MRT line. It has buses serving the short and long route in this area.
It has a Buzz shop where you can get a quick drink, snack or newspaper.
At the end of the interchange, there is. NTWU canteen catering food for the bus drivers and commuters as well.
There is a passenger service counter where you can make enquiries (The staff which I met today at 9.45am 11/Jan 2022 is quite helpful and friendly . Disclaimer: not all staff are so.lol) or simple head down to the bus service guides information board. You can scan the information for respective bus routes. Eg. Bus 61, scan a digital version of the route or alternatively you may pick up an information booklet as well if you require a print copy.
There is a security post nearby where the security personnel is constantly on patrol to ensure safety of commuters.
Buses are on time and frequency is really good.

Well done to our Singapore Bus services!

one bus，two bus ，three 77 buses had finished its round and back to interchange. none of the buses are moving 😐.waited for 40mins damn and decided to ride another bus. Plus larh if no bus ，just don‘t put any time on the screen ～taking forever to wait 🤷🏻
When I took Bus 947 to Dunearn Sec School alighted Blk 419 yesterday afternoon from my friend Haziq. But is okay to wait around 5 to 10 minutes in BBBI.

Is happen today when approved to passenger service counter (bukit  batok bus interchange) ask bus number 991 where to drop at which bus stop nearest the St LuKes hospital, The counter duty personnel ask me to ask the bus captain, So what is passenger service centre stand for,if reluctance to excess computer and give the details.The person is no busy at that times,just seat infront of computer.

My mother lost her wallet in the 106 bus and I contacted the help center. They told me that they found it and I could picked up the wallet here. It was only 1 hour since she lost the wallet. Everything still inside her wallet and they refuse the tip. Shout out to all singapore citizens and workers for their honesty.
Spacious and clean but sometimes the bus timings from this interchange are way off like 106 or 990 and it's really inconvenient if it says arriving in 3min but it actually arrives in 12min
Easy to navigate. Many buses to different parts of Singapore. Convenient shops, eateries and West Mall near by.

Bus arriving time for bus timing is not fix, normally is earlier and by the time walk to the berth, bus is already gone.should show ARRIVE instead. Today the bus 947 driver pretend did not see me and just left like that,was in front of the bus.

My son left a bag on the bus. It is quite valuable with a pair of nice football boots inside.

Emailed customer service at 7am and got a reply by 9am that the bag was found and can be collected at the bus interchange. Very impressed with how quiet the email response was, and also the bag was found.

This relatively new foodcourt offers great variety including few halal stalls. It's comfortable to dine there as the foodcourt is well ventilated and spacious. The highlights for me are the Vietnamese pho & banh mi, Prawn hokkien mee, Taiwanese braised pork rice, and local braised duck kueh teow/ rice.

While finally Bukit Batok got it's hawker centre, this is essentially a Food Court without air-conditioning. The number of food stalls(20+) pales compare to traditional hawkers that even only one available drink stall seems enough.

With all said, the food centre is airy with its high ceiling and non-congested table arrangements. They are good food amid its limited selections with Lor Mee my favorite.

I went to the fishball noodle and fish soup stall. Based on the menu the fish soup costs $6 for fish soup/fish porridge/fish beehoon. After I ordered the staff told me $13. So I asked how come additional charge. She said if normal $6 the fish very little only and said usually got noodle inside one so little. So I feedback should inform customers about it. They were not happy and face changed when I said I want $6 amount. Then they kept emphasising fish very little. I was like fine okay I just paid 50 cents more. Look at the pic how the sliced fish soup is like. I am just not so happy with how they just add $0.50 with the assumption of customers and themselves find it little really surprised me.

I will never patronised again.

Nice to eat were the duck rice stall, fishball noodle stall, wonton mee stall.

Not so nice were the chicken rice stall that had not fresh chicken, and newly opened japanese korean stall which we ate bibimbap and cutlet chicken with salmon with watery miso.

Totally love that there are huge fans that help provide some cooling in the hot weather! Ventilation is good. There are basins for washing of hands. Nearby playgrounds good to entertain the kids while we eat. The drinks were bad though, teh O kosong was so diluted tasted like water. Fried hokkien mee was too sweet!

Beef slices for vietnamese pho were overcooked so they were quite tough to chew on. The broth was fine. I think the best was the chicken rice set. Rice was fragrant and chicken breast wasn't too dry. Chili was very spicy, I wish they have spring onion ginger sauce too. Wanton mee, bak chor mee, sheng jiao bao and chicken wings were very good. Overall, delicious and affordable!
So, so. Rojak is traditional but nice. Hokkien mee ok, ok on the salty side. Others, not trying yet. Two coffee shops. Food hub. The other half is Broadway coffee shop

Spacious food centre that offers variety of food choices. Tried the chang chen chicken rice and they were very generous with their portion. The chicken rice was affordable as well, and comes with a free bowl of soup. Halal food options are available here as well!  Would highly recommend the chicken rice stall

A large coffee shop with many food stores. Several of them are supposedly famous, where you will see people queueing to buy or place order. Examples are the Fried Hokkien Mee store and the Lor Mee store. Spacious, breezy and clean. One of the better coffee shops I had visited.

A rather large kopitiam sitting side by side with another large coffeeshop - Broadway.

The noteworthy stalls are: Tiong Bahru lor mee and nasi lemak stalls that has super long queues, black and white fried carrot cake, teochew kuehs.

Honey Sea Coconut And Nata De Coco (SGD $3.50) @ Hundred Flavours Desserts.
.
When quality of ingredients is mostly standard (since it's all canned or processed), the other factors to compete on are portion size, price, service quality, convenient location, or creativity.
.
It's all about convenient location for this stall, with a largely captive audience that goes for takeaway or delivery.
.
Their rendition is refreshing. The slippery honey sea coconut and springy coconut water jelly, along with the smooth shaved ice and lime juice, combine for fruity floral earthy sour zesty sweet flavour.
.
Ice Jelly Fruit Cocktail (SGD $3.50) @ Hundred Flavours Desserts.
.
Their rendition is refreshing. The base of wobbly aiyu jelly and smooth shaved ice with lime juice, topped with juicy fruit cocktail (peach, pear, green grapes, longan), has a lovely floral fruity zesty sour sweet flavour.

really nice to-go matcha here! it was whisked fresh when i arrive and the aroma was amazing ☺️ matcha itself was not astringent/bitter at all, paired nicely with the strawberry compote 😗

the owner was really friendly to talk to, elevating the experience here 💯 highly recommend this to anyone who’s a matcha lover!

Strawberry matcha: One of the best we've ever had, the strawberry wasnt overpowering and had a nice texture. Good, thick matcha taste.

Matcha: As with above, strong and pronounced matcha taste. Not like other diluted matchas.

There is only takeaway. Can order in person but best to order online. Quick preparation.

Tried the classic matcha and strawberry matcha, and both were good. The matcha itself is smooth and not too bitter 👍🏼 the strawberry also adds a light, refreshing sweetness that complements the drink well. Will be back :)

Had a nice chat with the very friendly owner and its evident that he’s also a matcha enjoyer. I ordered the strawberry matcha. The matcha was smooth and rich and paired really well with the strawberry jam, creating a drink that is not too sweet while still tasting great.

Overall, definitely a great matcha place to visit when coming nearby.
easy going and chill environment. Drinks are delightful and worth trying. I would also recommend trying the yuzu matcha if you want a refreshing drink to start up your day.😇

place_name: MADRAS COFFEE HOUSE’S - Bukit Batok outlet
review_text: Very much recommend this cosy eatery. Food is fresh tasty. Tea coffee rich fulfilling.

place_name: MADRAS COFFEE HOUSE’S - Bukit Batok outlet
review_text:
No seating
Standing only

place_name: MADRAS COFFEE HOUSE’S - Bukit Batok outlet
MADRAS COFFEE HOUSE’S - Bukit Batok outlet
review_text:
This shop is another branch of Madras Coffee house.
They have snacks, sweets, cakes and quick food like dosa, parata, idly, vada, utappam.
The taste is good and food is catered from their main kitchen.

Some of the foods are good, except for this round, felt cheated. Ordered habib hokkien mee (white). Personally asked if this is chinese style hokkien mee & they confirmed it. To my dissappointment, it’s not. Not only d dish looks like hong kong noodle, taste as well. If any specific dish not in yr listed menu, pls dont indicate as such

Was in their neighbourhood and decided to try their HALAL beef ku teh. Good dining spot with plenty of dishes on their menu to choose from. The beef was tender but wished the soup was abit more “ooof”. Salted egg chicken and stir fried bean sprouts were alright too. Will probably come again to try other things on their menu.

Staff are lazy. I walked up to a staff to order. He told me to go to the counter to order. But No one was at the counter to take the order. Stood there for a while while the staff was chatting at the corner. Did not manage to order in the end. Table was dirty too and no one bothered to clean. Dont bother coming here.

i usually get their spicy fried rice w salted egg or petai fried rice and it always hits the spot!! their portion is ALOT for 1 definitely can last you throughout the day!! this time tried their charsiew chicken, ok ok onieee. Will stick to my usual order!

Situated at Bukit Gombak and jammed between a few other restaurants, Habib has made a name for themselves.

Wanted to makan dishes and so we decided on Habib. My sis loves this place for their butter naan and normally when I'm here, I would just almost always have individual dishes. However today was different decided to try individual dishes and boy it was a smart choice.

Sambal Petai, Black Pepper Beef, Sambal Sotong and Beancurds were all super sedap lah! Delicious!!!! Simple mouth watering. I can't gush enough on how good it was. And the portion size was absolutely fair. I don't care if I sound bias but it really was that good. And service timing was also on point. Maybe it was off peak but we had our drinks in less then 10 minutes and another 10 minutes all our orders were served.

My only grouse would be that a clearer instructions about the ordering process and the clearing if tables be put in place.

All in all, I would definately be here again and hopefully they keep up to their standards! Pls do not falter or regress cause that would be absolutely disappointing.

Food : 4 / 5
Staff : 3 / 5
Ambience : 3 / 5

Didn't know there was an eatery here, and since it's named "famous" decided to try it out.

The portion matched the description (small or regular etc) but the prices were on the slightly high side for the medium portion for the rice and noodle dishes.

Extremely slow service and food came sporadically, but due to it being the dinner crowd and just 2 cooks (open view of the kitchen) that was probably the case.

My friend ordered (from what I think it was from what was served to us) the Moonlight Horfun, Seafood Ee fu Noodles, Fried Rice, hotplate beancurd, Garlic and Honey Pork Ribs, Fu Yong Omelette, Prawn Paste Chicken. basically from the photos you can see what it was, and pardon me if I got rhe dish wrong.

Honestly, the food verdict? Not really worth it. The Garlic and Honey pork ribs was not bad, tender and juicy, and the Moonlight Hor Fun had wok hei, but that was it. The hor fun itself seemed plain with more kway teow and beansprouts than ingredients. Ee fu noodles was pretty standard, but no wok hei or standout flavor.

Hotplate beancurd was ok. quite standard tasting.

The veggies were tasty, but the portion was tiny.

The Egg Fu Yong was quite devoid of flavor as well.

The freeflow ice-cream though, was a nice touch on a hot day.

Staff were helpful and friendly though.

The worst was the Fried Rice which was soggy and had no flavor.

Sunday lunch on Mother's day, our first time here figured out the location behind the drop off lobby of the tower side. The aluminium roof is hot, giving the ambience of kampong. It is open space concept with roof. We had beehoon lala, fish head curry, coffee rib, spinach and 3 eggs, fried beansprout. Beehoon is fragrant and not salty. Spinach with 3 eggs is good, can taste saltiness from salted egg. Chicken with spices tasted great with fragrant of black pepper. Free ice cream is a nice treat. Pork rib and curry are slightly too sweet for my liking.

Had a nice dinner here. Very value for money. Good food, child playing area, free flow ice cream, a stage for band and magician. Great service.

Hokkien noodle was flavourful and full of ingredients, worth the price

Prawn paste chicken was not oily

The lala beehoon is highly recommended. Always order this whenever we visit. I loved the fish head curry as well. Those are my favorite and plenty of good choices for entire family.

This is the place we always go for a good comfortable zichar dinner. Reasonable price and very comfortable seating - not super crowded like some kopitiam places.

The service was great despite being a busy night - and a couple of service staff checked back a few times to see how we find the food.

Overall a great zichar restaurant

Excellent service, crew actually advise us to chose another option from what we ordered to avoid duplicate dishes. Food waiting was less than 15 mins even with crowds. I vote so far my best Zhi Char in the west comparing to other places. Price and food portion so reasonable from what I shown in the photos. Folks who read this post should really come and try yourself. There's even a small area for kids to play while adult dine, parking also convenient.

Whisk & Paddle offers a refreshing escape from the city, tucked away beside the greenery of Bukit Gombak Park. The peaceful setting creates a relaxing atmosphere that is perfect for breakfast, brunch or a leisurely coffee break.
The menu features a good variety of café favourites, with generous portions and consistently satisfying flavours. The coffee is well made, and the desserts are worth saving room for. What makes the experience especially enjoyable is the tranquil environment, where you can unwind while enjoying views of the surrounding greenery.
Service is friendly and attentive, and the spacious layout makes it comfortable even during busier periods. A charming neighbourhood café that combines good food, a pleasant ambience and a welcome connection to nature. Highly recommended.
Piccolo latte!

We spotted this charming glasshouse tucked away amidst the greenery at Bukit Gombak Park, and the best part? It’s just a 7-minute drive from my ulu workplace — practically a miracle! There were 2 dishes that I simply had to order: The Macaroni and Cheese in pink sauce — rich, creamy and flavourful without being overly jelat. The French Toast — crispy on the outside, soft and fluffy on the inside, complemented by sea salt butter and maple syrup.

Great brunch spot with a cozy vibe. The French toast with bacon and scrambled eggs was a standout—crispy, fluffy, and super satisfying. Pastas were solid too, and the coffee hit the spot. Friendly service and a really chill atmosphere. Would definitely come back.

Was welcomed very warmly by JJ — such a great first impression! All the staff here are genuinely friendly and attentive, which really makes this café stand out. JJ even went out of the way to wipe down the chair for my child with antibacterial wipes — that’s really going above and beyond what most places would do.

The food was quite good too — tasty, nicely presented, and served promptly. There was also an event going on with balloons for children, which I thought was a really nice touch. It made the whole place feel cheerful and family-friendly.

Overall, Whisk & Paddle at Bukit Gombak Park is a lovely spot with great food, warm service, and a welcoming atmosphere for families. Highly recommended!

Ordered the smoked salmon toast and chicken and waffles.

The fried chicken was nicely seasoned and crispy without feeling overly greasy.

The smoked salmon was paired with avocado and a nice butter toast. It was also accompanied by 2 poached eggs. The whole combo was delicious.

The matcha strawberry was a nice drink without being overly sweet.

The cheesecake was blowtorched at our table. The nice crust complemented the cheesecake.

The environment was chill as well.

Secret gem in the west.
The breakfast platter has improved - really good quality sausage and thick bacon. Huge portions

Beef brisket was super yummy and generously thick slices.

Good value on entertainer

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Coexist Coffee Co. @Hillview is a weekend favorite. The staff are genuinely warm and friendly, making every visit feel welcoming and relaxed. Their big breakfast is a standout—generous portions, well‑cooked, and perfect for starting the day right. Pair it with their excellent coffee and you’ve got a spot that balances great food, great service, and a cozy atmosphere. Highly recommended for a satisfying weekend brunch.

I visited Co Exist Hillview for my yearly birthday breakfast, as I’ve enjoyed previous visits, but this time the experience was very disappointing.

The coffee was fine, and visually the dishes looked good (photos attached). Unfortunately, the taste and execution did not match the presentation at all.

The fish and chips had a very hard, dry batter, and the salad was just plain greens — missing items that were previously standard, such as pickled onions, cherry tomatoes, and even a lemon wedge. The laksa sauce was extremely thick and tasted like it had been reheated or improperly prepared.

The mala wings were also not what was described on the menu. The batter was similarly dry and hard, and the sauce leaned sweet with no noticeable mala flavour at all.

Their signature Co Exist breakfast was another letdown. The sausage appears to have changed and was very dry, and the eggs were overcooked and no longer creamy, which is very different from what I’ve had in previous years.

I raised this politely with the staff on duty, who offered a refund on the foods after checking with the manager. I was told the manager would call me, but no one followed up.

Overall, the value for money was very poor. Neighbourhood western stalls could deliver better-tasting food at 50–60% lower of the price. I don’t usually leave negative reviews, but I feel it’s only fair to be honest so customers know what to expect. I hope management reviews consistency, menu accuracy, and quality control, as this was not the standard I remembered — especially for a birthday visit.

Hidden gem with seriously good vibes and food to match. Tucked away in an industrial area, this place has that minimalist, slightly “secret café” feel that makes the visit a bit more special.

Ordered the sandwich with fries and a coffee — both delivered. The sandwich was hearty and well-balanced (loved the soft bread with the creamy filling and fresh toppings), and the fries were crispy and addictive. The coffee was smooth and well-executed, with nice latte art to top it off ☕️

Service was friendly and efficient, and the overall atmosphere was relaxed but still lively — great spot for a casual brunch or catching up with friends.

Definitely a place I’d come back to, especially if you enjoy good café food in a unique setting.

Interesting location, great food and drinks! I thoroughly enjoyed our meal here. All dishes were well executed with generous portions. They can accomodate kids too, with a wide rooftop area to get the wiggles out, if necessary.

Cleanliness and littering a big issue. Enforcement slacking. The local wet market is thriving with rodents. We don't need an epidemic to start doing something about it. Can the local council please pull up your socks and get accountable.
The barber vishwa is very good and I gotten the exact hairstyle I wanted

Chance upon this small shopping centre after visiting my mom at nearby St Luke Hospital. Quite a small area with many oldies shops around. You get all you need in the small areas though. Good foods around.

A small-scaled shopping centre located in the heart of Bukit Batok neighborhood. There are quite a few shops here and it also has a carpark too. There's also a coffee shop which looks quite newly recommended here. A good place to sit down and rest before exploring nearby.

Very nice neighbourhood shops and coffeeshops to walk about
Baker has very nice pandan chiffon cakes fresh from the oven
Tailor cum clothing shop sells wrinkle free short sleeve shirts
The tailor even gave advice on which range of short sleeve shirts that would better suit my size and body shape
Bought ~12 cm tall plant pots suitable for HDB corridor plants not too big from Sheng Siong
Wet market stall selling roast pork and braised duck has cold pork jelly 猪脚冻 cheap n good
Yam kueh from the coffeeshop stall highly recommended

Fantastic mall in Bukit Batok with a variety of stores that caters to all needs. From fashion wear to electrical, Korean snacks, bb tea and a beautiful spacious Kopitiam foodcourt to have some rojak and desserts. Bukit Batok Liibrary here as well to read and explore.

They recently just completed a massive upgrading program.  The mall is completely transformed. It's pretty and has more eateries and shops compared to before. Looks much more high end than before. the F&B section is attractive. will definitely come back to try it out the eateries.

Just went to the mall, and not much has changed, the escalator still down. Went to the food court which was renovated, seems more spacious now, but would think that food options pretty similar to most. Though its Kopitiam, seems pretty similar to Gourmet Paradise at Tengah which also has Yuen Kee and similar western stall. B1 still has more food options, but see the snacks stall that has closed and has been closed for quite awhile already.

Many food options in newly opened basement 1 and best bakery choices in level 1. This is my go to place while walking back from MRT.

Level 2 and 3 are filled with many other shopping options like Japan Home, Value dollar, Popular, Harvey Norman.

It has been a while since I was here at West Mall - that is before its basement level one food zone opened, or in some cases, reopened. Anyway, I was here today visiting the food zone for the first time and I must say that it is quite impressive in terms of the many and good eateries (restaurants and kiosks) now available and the very wide walkways thereat. That said, I am a bit sad that the Cathay Cineplex at its upper most floor is now permanently closed and that the Koufu food court on level four has also closed. Still, this heartland shopping mall is in my opinion quite relevant as it continues to refresh and updates itself with the times.

This supermarket is always busy in the morning. I had a pleasant experience this time as I was to find freshly replenished stock of chicken boneless legs which I came here for.  Bought it. That's was all I needed and I was in and out in 10 mins. Best shopping experience as this place is always jammed with shoppers and the queue at cashier are always long. Today it was so smooth.

With 20 people in que only 1 billing counter open at 1 pm on 1st Dec 2025
Staff available but all on break.
No one bothered to manage the customer que
No accountability

Tonight I visited this NTUC to grab a few last minute groceries and I was fortunate to have met the manager and his coworker who offered me their help. I wish for this message to reach them to say thank you for their kind gesture. They saw I was struggling with the bottles of mineral water and they didn't hesitate to approach me, I have to say that it made me really happy (:

Ms Yong Choon Kin one of the NTUC (Bukit Batok East branch)
Staff, Cashier.
I would like to give her my COMPLIMENTS
as she is Always Smiling, even though there is a Long Queue.

Thank YOU
😘
Looks crammed and chaotic. Staff is good. No self checkin. Cash Counters are slow. Many items were already out of stock at 4 pm .

Most of the cashiers are pretty friendly, they will recommend you what are the items on discount or certain items you will be able to buy 1 get 1 free promotions. Their staffs are well taken care of thus very strong family orientated environment are present. New products are also highlighted to customers giving more choices to choose from.

Sheng Siong Supermarket is a reliable shopping destination known for its affordable prices and wide variety of products. The store offers fresh produce, meat, and household essentials, making it a convenient one-stop shop. The layout is well-organized, allowing for easy navigation, even during busy hours. Staff are generally friendly and helpful, enhancing the shopping experience. While the store can get crowded, especially on weekends, the savings on everyday items make it worthwhile. Overall, Sheng Siong is a great choice for budget-conscious shoppers looking for quality goods in a welcoming environment.
Very easy to shop. Helpful staff. They have almost everything you need from wet to dry goods.

Shopping here is a pleasure! A wide selection of products, from fresh fruits to household items, all neatly arranged. Prices are fair, and weekly deals offer great savings. The staff is courteous and always ready to assist. It’s my go-to spot for all grocery needs!
They did not remove most of the fish scales. Have to do it myself. Messy and have to clean up. They should improve their service.

Great selection of household items here. It is located deep in the mall and takes a bit of walking to get to. Good to serve the nearby residents here apart from the sheng siong outlet located near the mall entrance.
Varieties of goods and produces. Price wise sometimes need to check to make sure it's not over priced. Sometimes it does as I experienced it before. But generally, NTUC fair price. :)....👍😊

Weekends are busier but only one cashier counter on the outside. Thankfully the lady cleared pretty fast so waiting is good as older people can't stand in line longer than expected with trollys. Two staffs will be better suggestion one inside one outside. What you think.

I understand its the Chinese New Year therefore the store would not be able to get stocks as frequent to maintain its freshness. However, planning of stocking dry goods is as important as fresh goods. Most shelves are empty and its affecting the shopping experience. Inventory could be improve.

Anyhow pack items into my recycle bag and say is full and ask me want to buy plastic a not. In the end packed myself and get everything in. Such attitude won’t happen in Sheng Siong, duno why most ntuc aunties are so menapause? Should have gone to nearby Sheng Siong instead.

Dropped by to pick up some batteries. Do note to bring cash if you anticipate your purchase to be below $5, and NETS is only accepted if your purchase is above that amount. Paynow is also not accepted either.
the plump chinese lady staff at the register is so warm and friendly. even on a busy day where customers are rushing? wow shes my inspiration for providing good customer service

Very Disappointing Experience – DAAWAT Rozana Basmati Rice

On 22nd May 2025, I purchased DAAWAT Rozana Basmati Rice from ABC Bargain Centre located at 371 Bukit Batok Street 31, #01-312, Singapore 650371. Near Bukit Gobak MRT.

Unfortunately, the product turned out to be extremely disappointing.

Upon opening the bag and using it for cooking, I noticed that the rice quality was far from what is expected of genuine Basmati rice. The grains were small, broken, dull in colour, and lacked the typical aroma and texture associated with authentic Basmati rice. After cooking, the rice tasted bland and had none of the fragrance that makes Basmati unique.

This experience was very frustrating, especially when purchasing from a well-established store. Food quality is a serious matter, and such low-grade products should never be sold to consumers.

I urge the store management to re-evaluate and verify the quality of such products before placing them on their shelves. I have attached photos for reference.

The staff in this branch behave rude. I bought something $7.45 and i give her $10.05. She return me $2.50. I inform her is incorrect. She keep said is correct and talk with me loudly . Then i show her calculater how much should return. She like still not trust it and unwilling to return $0.10. Although is only $0.10 , i can let go but just cannot accept the attitude of the staff.

Very rude staff name Mariana or Marina, with a short hair. This kind of staff behaviour shouldnt be in this line of customer service!!! Very disappointing! Your management must take action on this!Customers beware of this staff!😡😡😡😡
Quaint and fairly priced neighborhood convenience store. Sells some fresh vegetable produce as well.

Went to the outlet (Tagore) to place an order for CNY, to make sure that delivery would be in place for reunion dinner.

Food was delivered just before the scheduled delivery time, which was exactly what we needed. Quality was good and tasted fresh. The fishballs and beef balls were especially well received.

Will use them again next time we do a steamboat.

What a great wet market, combined with Sheng Siong, lots of independent small stores, food court. The prices are fantastic compare to NTUC Fair price at the wet market. There’s heap of hawker food too, especially busy were prata, chicken rice, Lor mee and bee hoon and curry chicken. Nice atmosphere in the weekend and old nostalgia where your ah-Ma would love to shop here. Just wished it had more meat and vegetable stores in the wet market section. Good place to show the kids and spend the morning in the weekend.

I highly recommend this butcher!  Prime Pork ribs are very high quality, smooth, tender and fresh.  Their minced pork is the perfect meat/ fat ratio, very fresh.  No rancid smell at all.  I’ve also purchased their deboned chicken (quarter) 500gm pack many times- used it for grilling, steaming, braising.  Also recommend their pork meat balls- very tasty, has cuttlefish in it.  They deliver the items in an insulated box, full of ice packs to keep the meat cold and safe.  Everything is vacuum packed.  I’m so pleased with their service.  Their pricing is also competitive.  The thing with going to the wet market physically, you still need to repack into your desired portions upon coming home, so ordering from quan shui really saves me a lot of time and energy.

I always love going to the wet markets but cannot afford the time to wake up super early to get the freshest produce! Love that they deliver to your doorstep and even give the option to recycle the ice packs/boxes. The selection of produce is great, quality is really good and worth every bit of the slight premium!! Fruits & meat are super fresh! Ready to eat section is great for quick meals too! 100% will recommend!

We cooked with the wild caught balai threadfin for porridge, Silverfern short rib shabu shabu for Korean beef Bulgogi, silverfern ribeye steak for Beef bowl. It was amazing! 👌

We’ve been buying from Quan Shui Wet Market for a few times, and each time a satisfying one. Their items are fresh and clean, reached us in good condition and packaging. Complimentary delivery is reliable and good! Joyce from customer service is friendly and helpful. So far we’ve purchased almost all types of what they are selling and we’ll still purchase from them for long. Thank you Quan Shui Market!

Never thought I will walk to this Bukit Gomak Neighbourhood Centre. Having quite a lot coffee shop. Found one operator by Kimly. They have Msia style zi char and worth trying ban mien and carrot cake.
There’s a prime market where you can buy affordable salmon fish heads, a variety of vegetables, and a beautiful park.

Convenient and has 2 affordable supermarkets giant and prime. It also has many local stalls, DIY stalls aka mana shop selling batteries home appliances etc. Love that there are 2 halal coffee shops and three Chinese coffee shops all in the vicinity. Connected to gombak Mrt and the Xiao Guo Lin park too! Even better, lots of greenary and playgrounds! For more, follow @modern_mulans on Instagram!

30 years of living at this neighbourhood. I gote to see it all. From the first shops opened, the building of the bt gombak mrt, to the building of the large square that jave hosted many bazaar. I know every face of the shopkeepers. They have shaped the market landscape and formed the heart of bt gombak. Many of their children have taken over their parents businesses, but it is still the same. Come and visit and see how wonderful this place is.

Like this neighbourhood shopping area. Plenty of consumer friendly sundry shops and coffeeshop. Can buy anything from household electrical items, TCM medicines, mobile phones to grocery at supermarkets. I like it.

The gym environment was motivating and welcoming. The equipment was well-maintained, and there was a good variety of machines and weights available for different fitness goals. The staff and trainers were friendly, approachable, and willing to help when needed. I also appreciated the cleanliness of the facility and the positive atmosphere, which made workouts enjoyable and encouraged consistency.

Syafiq was very helpful and friendly, and made the whole visit feel welcoming and comfortable. He took the time to assist and answer questions without any pressure, which I really appreciated.

The gym itself is clean, spacious and the equipment is well maintained. Overall, a very positive environment to train in. Highly recommended!

I had a good experience at the gym. The equipment is in good condition, the space is clean, and the atmosphere is great for workouts. The staff are friendly and welcoming too. I would definitely recommend others to sign up here.

Personally, I think the gym space is quite good. Although it is on the smaller side, it is still decent enough for beginners or casual gym-goers who are looking to get into fitness. To be fair, if you are a serious bodybuilder or someone who requires a wide range of specialised equipment to target specific muscle groups, this gym may not fully meet your needs. However, for those who are simply aiming for a healthier lifestyle, improved confidence, or general fitness, it serves its purpose reasonably well.

My main feedback is more towards the management, as I feel more could be done to improve the overall member experience.

To elaborate, here are a few areas where I believe there is room for improvement:

1. Building a stronger gym community
For example, AF Joo Koon seemed to recognise the challenge of its location and made a visible effort to create a welcoming environment for members. They were fortunate to have a larger space, which allowed them to organise classes and activities to engage members. I understand that AF Bukit Batok Connection may not have the same advantage, and that is no one’s fault. However, beyond facilities, it is the effort from the staff that stands out. At Joo Koon, even by my third visit, the manager took the initiative to strike up a conversation and make me feel welcome.
To AF BBC’s credit, they implemented personal trainers here, and are friendly and give off warm, approachable vibes. I am always happy to see them around.

2. Creating a sense of belonging for members
I feel the outlet could do more to foster a stronger identity among its members. For instance, having AF Bukit Batok Connection’s own merchandise, such as a T-shirt or even limited seasonal designs, could help members feel more connected to the gym. In the same way people enjoy wearing activewear brands they identify with, outlet-specific merchandise could create a sense of pride and belonging. Personally, I think it would help cultivate that feeling of being a proud member of the gym.

3. Increasing visibility and engagement on social media
I follow AF BBC on both Instagram and TikTok, but most of the Anytime Fitness content I come across tends to be from other outlets. It does not always have to be promotional content, even simple posts showing the staff’s personalities, fun ideas, or light-hearted but safe interactions could go a long way. More active and authentic social media engagement could better reflect the passion of the staff and the warmth of the gym community, regardless of the outlet’s size.

Overall, this is a decent gym with a good foundation, but I do hope the management can do more to strengthen the member experience and build a stronger sense of community.

Anytime Fitness Bukit Batok (Le Quest) may not be one of the largest AF outlets in Singapore, but it does a lot with the space it has

The gym is relatively compact, yet it feels clean, well-maintained and thoughtfully laid out. The strength training area is equipped with squat racks, free weights and the essential machines needed for a full-body workout. Despite the smaller footprint, I was able to complete my workout without much difficulty

I particularly appreciated the cleanliness of the facility. The equipment was in good condition, the floors were tidy, and the overall environment felt welcoming and comfortable

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Amazing gym, even better staff support!
I’ve had a great experience at Anytime Fitness. The gym is clean, well-maintained, and very well equipped — from cardio machines to free weights and strength equipment. The layout is spacious and comfortable, making workouts enjoyable even during busier hours.
Special shout-out to Staff Diniy for the excellent service and support. Diniy is friendly, professional, and always willing to help, whether it’s guidance on equipment or just creating a welcoming gym atmosphere. That kind of staff attitude really makes a big difference.
Highly recommended for anyone looking for a convenient, well-run gym with great people behind it. 💪🔥

I've been a member here since last month and I'm really liking it so far. The gym is compact but clean and well-maintained, with all the essential equipment you’d expect—from free weights to cardio and strength machines.

Even though it’s not a large space, it doesn’t feel too crowded most of the time. The 24/7 access is super convenient, and the location inside Le Quest makes it easy to combine workouts with errands or meals.

The staff are approachable and helpful during staffed hours, and the environment feels welcoming for both new and experienced gym-goers. A solid choice for anyone living in the area or looking for a quiet and convenient place to train.

Adjust your expectations of a commercial gym, it’s closer to a decent-to-very good “hotel gym setup” instead given the comically small size and cramped layout. Really can’t give more than 3 stars. 3.5 maybe.

To be fair, the facility is reasonably well maintained, but it simply doesn’t match the standards of newer, more modern Anytime Fitness outlets. I’d be keen to know whether there are plans to take over the adjacent units to expand and bring it up to par.

Oh, and the white fluorescent lighting. If 90s hospital aesthetics are your thing, you’ll probably enjoy it here.
Love this gym! Clean space, great equipment, and friendly staff. Never too crowded, and the vibe keeps me motivated. Perfect spot for a solid workout! 💪✨

Loved playing here. Very nice, good courts, wooden flooring. Maintained well. Water leaked is swiftly cleaned by the keeper/janitor added signboard. Ventilation and air conditioning was good, without affecting the shuttle drifts.

Badminton court, basketball court, and bigger size gym, water dispenser for drinking water. I love here but is really hard to book the court from active sg as there's only 6 indoor badminton courts.

Walking distance from bukit gomabak mrt.
Refer active sg website for any updates.

Once of the dirtiest sport hall in Singapore. We booked 2 courts. But my friend who book the other court was sick n couldn't come. N staff insisted that we can only play on 1 court as the other court was sunder his name n he didn't come. Yet, they say then cannot refund the fees even though he was sick. But insist that he comes... sign in then we can use the court. They don't seem to understand that he was sick..that's why he couldn't come..n insisted he come.

Tried to play table tennis at an outdoor TTX table there. Good enough for sweating out and normal strokes practice provided that you have strong stamina for picking balls flying further away since no ball blockers. Also, very, very, very low chance of net balls due to the iron net barrier; rather the balls either bounce back or bounce out high at net hit. One plus point during our play is almost no ball-disrupting wind; may be the evening timing is the reason. The hill greenery and scenery at the background is also a refreshing change compared to indoor table tennis halls.

Spacious & well laid out area. Loved the greenery surrounding the sports hall.
Enjoyed the Mid-Autumn festival event that was held there. Only if there were more parking spots to go around, it would have been super fabulous!

My first day is here at activesg Bukit batak gombak, I liked it  peaceful area place there are a lot of new things for the gym and the special lady Ann Madam has been very helpful to me, She is understanding very well and lovingly. I love this place and behavior very well thank Ann I want to join continue this place and I will recommend everyone come to place It is a very peaceful place and there is a competition to do gym. I did it for 2 hours. I wanted to spend more time there, but I got a call for some work. Then I had to leave in 2 hours. I am hoping. I will come again and spend more time there. Thank you so much Ann
I am very disappointed with this gym. The woman at the front desk kept bothering me throughout my workout; it almost seemed as if she was doing it on purpose. I will not be coming back.
Nice but small gym. Does not have a cable row machine

review_author_name: သတ်တော်ရှည် သီရိပဝရဝဇယာနန္တယသပဏ္ဍိတ တြိဘဝနအာဒိတျာ မဟာဓမ္မရာဇာဓိ
review_text: Gym is ok but the staff is a little rude to me

Worst active Sg outlet I have been, the gym goer there chop/booked multiple equipments with their towels.

There is this SCDF old man in the morning especially, and when asked to share he gave attitude and raised his voice saying "he is still using" then proceed to use other machine with his towel continue booking the machine I asked for.

I complaint to the staff about the frequent chopping/booking incident of but she just stood there, laugh and walked away.

Fyi I have been going to gombak gym for 10 years or so and never once encounter this.

For those whom want to visit avoid the 7am to 9am slot because this is the period where all the self entitled people visit from my experience.

Bukit Batok Swimming Complex is located within the HDB neighbourhood.

I went to swim there during the weekend. Do note that there are some lanes being used for training.

The swimming pool's facilities are well maintained and clean so far. There's a decent pool area for the kids.

The shower head is very powerful. Similar to other public swimming pools, there's no heater. There's toilet specially for kids. For the female toilet, there's this adjustable hair dryer, which is pretty cool. I don't need to hold it while it dries my hair. You need to put your hand over the sensor for it to sense, and it will start blowing. However, it might stop after a while, so you need to wave your hand again for it to work.

There are lockers if you want to lock your belongings. If not, you can put it along the seats of the pool.

There's also water cooler with icy water for you to refill your bottle or drink directly.

Overall, it's a good experience swimming in Bukit Batok Swimming Complex.

Complex has been around for many decades, almost iconic. There is a fun pool for children too! HDB parkkng available nearby.
Unfortunately, there is only 1 entrance to the complex. So always have to walk one big round if parked at nearby HDB.

- S$1.3 for adult on weekdays.
- The changing room is clean and spacious.
- It is a little far from changing room to competition pool, a little crowded on Monday evening
- There are 3 types of pool: competition, training and kid.
Great swimming class for kids. Coach is patient, friendly, and professional. My kids gained confidence and improved quickly. He also looks forward to the water playground after class. Recommended.

I would not recommend to go there when you go there first time and let them know you are visitor. They treat you differently when we enter before paying everything we told them we are visitor coming first time and nobody informed us about swimming suits, but after we paid suddenly, they let us know that you need swimming suit. This is like a totally scam and taking out the money they could have told us before entering instead of telling us after paying I would not recommend at all the staff was not cooperating at all

Nice and serene place. Place is rather dated but well maintained, though it does show its age. There is a simple variety of activities that can be done here, though it is a common place for functions and meetings.

Civil Service Club Bukit Batok swimming pool.

A variety of fun and relaxing water activities, but access is limited to members and their guests.

The pool area includes a large children's pool, and a wave pool that starts at 10am and runs for 5 minutes every hour—check the website for the schedule. For $3 in cash, you can rent a float, which is required for the towel slide.

The lazy river provides a nice spot to lounge, while kids can enjoy the water playground and a simple water slide on the second level. Swimmers also have access to a lap pool located upstairs.

Hot water showers, hair dryer, and locker available.
Service was good, portion was really big. Ordered 2 small noodles, small veg, small sweet and sour pork and small tofu for 4 people and we can't finish half the amount of food.

I love this club as it’s a really huge place for families gathering. There are a few swimming pool cater for kids and adults. The awesome wave pool is well liked by the members.

The bathroom is also well designed and provides much comfort for members.

The whole premise is clean and greenery!
vc

Great environment, hillside club with surrounding nature scenery.
Pool facilities and amenities are club standard.
Slide pool is down for maintenance indefinitely.
Wave pool starts from 10am every 1hr intervals or at such times where the personnel deem appropriate.

Lifeguard stationed at wave pool has a working fan, but subjected to availability of sunlight on solar panel or has restricted power source?

Guest pay $2 entrance. Changing room locker $1.

First time at EagleWings Cinematics, after having a great dinner at EagleWings Loft a few months back.

First impressions were excellent. The staff warmly showed us around the lounge and explained how we could enjoy the place before the movie. We were especially excited to order the lobster risotto again, which we had been craving.

The lounge experience was affected when a rowdy group arrived before what seemed to be a company movie screening. Not the cinema’s fault, but it would help if staff could gently guide such groups to available seats instead of allowing them to block walkways and inconvenience other guests.

Our sides arrived quickly, though the risotto took a little longer. Staff were attentive throughout and checked with us on whether we preferred to dine in the lounge or in the hall.

The Platinum Hall was beautifully furnished and comfortable. Our food was neatly arranged at our seats when we entered. One thing to note is that the top-row “sofa” seats do not have centre armrests, unlike the lower rows, which may matter depending on seating preference.

Before the movie started, a family of three occupied two sofa sections, and the child was climbing around and making quite a bit of noise. Thankfully, it stopped once the movie began after the finally parents stepped in. It did make me wonder whether there are checks to ensure guests are seated according to their bookings.

Overall, it was still a very enjoyable evening, and I would gladly return again, hopefully without another large corporate screening crowd while in the lounge, or rowdy children in the theatre.

Made a trip today to try out EagleWings Cinematics for the first time, and overall, it was a pleasant experience. While we initially encountered some hiccups with the lounge service, the manager and team were proactive, sincere, and prompt in addressing our concerns, which we truly appreciated. The theatre itself was well-maintained, with spacious and comfortable seating. Dining in the theatre was enjoyable, and the Loft Menu offered good food and snacks at reasonable prices. Given the many positive aspects of our visit, we look forward to returning.

Everything was good but the service of the food counter staff on duty today. We ordered popcorn from the QR code in our seats, but after 15-20 mins it still had not arrived. We went out and saw that your staff Madison who was at the popcorn counter was playing with her phone and did not take notice of our order. Re-training highly encouraged.

Amazing experience. We booked in advance the premium lounge. My son and I used their massage chairs. That alone was worth the experience. It was like getting a deep tissue massage. They seem to have upgraded their massage chairs recently. Oh and free flowing drinks (hot or hold). (I recommended u come at least 45mins before the cinema start time). Then we went in, each two people can sit together on a couch which reclines and allows you to stretch your legs. It even comes with a fresh blanket in a pouch. Then u can scan the QR code and order as if you’re at a restaurant with a garment menu. Optionally you can also order popcorn 🍿and pay as u order. or press a separate call button to order more free soft drinks.

Royal experience. We watched supermario galaxy movie and the sound system and the screen size was suitable for the size of the room.

I realized that we picked the best cinema in SG when we kept talking about how awesome the experience for 10mins while we walked back to the MRT. We started planning our next visit as soon as we walked out.

Oh and the kindness and the hospitality is something else. This great level of treatment of customers is a rare treat in Singapore.

I wish I can give it higher than 5 stars
⭐️ ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ it’s obvious whoever came up with this idea loves cinema, food and wants to share their passion with other people in SG. I wish this place more success and expansion in SG.

Kitty and I have been loyal patrons of EagleWings Cinematics for years, and we absolutely adore this cinema.  It's a truly unique experience that we keep coming back to again and again.

EagleWings Cinematics is located within the KAP Mall, and it stands out from other cinemas in Singapore because of its intimate and luxurious atmosphere.  Each theater is cozy and designed to hold a small group of people, like 12 to 20 people, making it feel like a private screening. It's so comfortable and relaxing!  The seating is fantastic, and we've tried all their classes—Silver, Gold, and Platinum.  Our favorites are Gold and Platinum, which offer plush reclining sofas with blankets to keep you warm.

But the real highlight is their amazing service and amenities.  Right next to the cinema is a fantastic restaurant where you can order a wide variety of food, from local favorites like laksa to international dishes like steak and fish and chips.  You can even order beer if you choose Gold or Platinum class.  It's a far cry from the typical cinema snacks! You can also enjoy their lounge one hour before your movie starts, with free-flowing coffee and soft drinks, and even two massage chairs to relax in!

The service is impeccable.  They efficiently deliver your food to your table in the theater, and the table lamps are perfectly positioned to illuminate your meal without disturbing other guests.  The sound system is excellent, and the air conditioning is just right.  It's a comfortable and enjoyable movie-going experience.

We love how EagleWings never feels crowded or rowdy.  The atmosphere is always so peaceful and relaxing, perfect for enjoying a movie.  We've recommended EagleWings to many of our friends, and they've all become addicted to this amazing cinema.  From that point on, they've been watching movies at EagleWings exclusively!

The overall experience is always positive.  It gives you a holistic experience, where you can enjoy your movie on the comfortable sofa seat and have delicious meals in the cinema.  The food we've tried over the years has always been above average.

EagleWings is also incredibly convenient.  All the theaters are on level 1, and if you're looking to book a private function, you can head to level 2.

We highly recommend EagleWings Cinematics to anyone looking for a luxurious and memorable cinema experience.  It's a great option for a romantic date night, a family outing, or a night out with friends.
Terribly run. Messy. Uncoordinated. Unhelpful staff members. The renovated space is even more inaccessible.

A very calm place where you can enjoy your time. The surroundings are quiet and the burger king here offers you some quality beef burgers so that you bring your comics and spend your leisure time after work. You can just kill your time by gazing at the traffic or just going for a walk around this place.

Temporarily closed, under construction.

Before that though, very smooth mask collection and TraceTogether token collection. There are also quite a lot of functions here with a decent variety of activities and programmes to take part.
Many activities every month for all ages! Gym, study room and IT room is available for public usage. Feeling hungry? Drop by the newly opened Burger King at level 1

Very rude and unfriendly staff serving at the TT tokens counter. I intended to change the batteries for my family, not knowing about there were actually a new and updated tokens being distributed. Staff demanded to have identification cards of my family. But I told that they already took my family details when I first took the 1st batch of TT tokens, and they should just check against their records. These staff told me if there is anything that I am not happy about, I can go straight to look for the MP.....WHAT A GREAT SERVICE FROM THEM.....

I recently accompanied my elderly mother to the Hong Kah Community Centre to resolve a Singpass issue. The customer service team was very kind and helpful from the start. When we went to the wrong department, the staff patiently guided us to the correct office next door. There, we were assisted by two lovely ladies, and I especially want to thank Ms. Charmaine Khoo. She was caring, professional, and attentive, taking the time to help my mother with great patience and understanding. After everything was settled, she even walked us out and kindly wished us a good day. Her warmth and professionalism made my mother very happy. Thank you to Charmaine and the team for their excellent service!
Best,
J and mum
I came here to get my SG60 voucher printed and the staff here are patience and amazing. Thank you Agnes and William for your excellent customer service
very friendly volunteers, staff and MP Amy Khor, always organise so many programs for the residents like us

A very active community club. Have seen basketball tournaments and playing sessions, badminton sessions, table tennis tournaments as well as occasional dance sessions at the ground floor halls as well as in the open space near Giant Supermarket. Two dance studios can be found at the third floor.

I have no words to express my feelings about a big dream of mine as I am not qualified for the project to serve the society. I have I hope we can train a group of befriended volunteers visiting their house and train a basic treatment therapy session for our BUKIT BATOK Area and I pray for if successful then we can have the whole nation of interest candidate who will learn a basic treatment therapy to help those people who are vulnerable, disabled and elderly people who having uncomfortable part in their body areas. I'm ASURE THAT IF I HAVE A INTENTION TO BE A PART OF THE TEAM TO SHARE A TREATMENT THERAPY, AND ALTHOUGH I'M DON'T HAVE A PROFESSIONAL CERTIFICATE. Please get it done

A peaceful and well-maintained neighbourhood park, perfect for a relaxing walk or some quiet time outdoors. The greenery is lush, and the paths are suitable for walking or light exercise. It’s a nice escape from the city bustle and a great spot for nearby residents to unwind and enjoy nature.

This super lovely park had been around since the late 80s i think. In recent years underwent renovation to give the amenity a new life-building extension bridges, landscaping for the nearby residents, more shelters area for people.

⛰️Bukit Batok Neighbourhood Park, aka Fuji Hill Park, is a short walk from Bukit Batok MRT Station.
🌳The once tired-looking hill park had recently been revamped into a vibrant multi-generational park.
🧗🛝🪅The revamped Hill Park features a rainbow tower long slide, an elevated boardwalk, a summit platform, community garden, Fitness Station, jogging tracks, children playgrounds and more.
💪🏃It's a lovely lush green Hill Park where residents nearby visit daily for their morning workout.
🍏Fun fact:
⛰️Bukit Batok and the nearby Bukit Gombak area were hilly terrains.
🪨During the development of Bukit Batok Town estate, in the late 1980s, Fuji Hill was recontoured to better fit to the surrounding area.
⛰️The conical shape grassy knoll without any trees look much like a mini version of Mt Fuji, and was monikered Fuji Hill.
🌳Presently, with structures and many tall trees covering the knoll, it's conical shape had blended in with the lush vegetations and are no longer obvious of its conical shape.
🌳⛰️Those who had lived in Bukit Batok since the 1980s would have fond memories of the conical shape Fuji Hill.

Bukit Batok Neighborhood Park..near Blk 204 Bukit Batok Central.. Short walk from Bukit Batok MRT station..walk past the NTUC supermarket at the ground floor of the station..down the escalator and turn LEFT AND walk along bukit batok central road..about 10 mins...cross the road towards blk 204. At the base of this HILL park, there is along flight of steps to get to the "nature bridge" a steel bridge built to walk along the cliff and large wood, plank surfaced   platform or deck ( some sheltered portions) to rest at the top of the "hill". It looks more like a resort!. A good view of the region from the top. A few senior citizens can be seen tending to the plants at the top. On a Saturday morning there were about 10 to 15 people exercising.

Newly opened playground at Bukit Batok Central Fuji Hill Park, the playground is spacious with numerous play structures such as long winding tube slides and rope obstacles. A fun place for kids while adults can jog around the park.
1st time visit.. Amazing...  if you are nature lover ,  this area is ok  for relaxation
There many fish in a pond at the 1st floor but as it's a heavy rain kids can't go to the swiming pool at 3rd floor, they just sit & waiting. I just went there for a wedding invitation..
Nice place for family & friends gathering
This is children playroom with children function room can be rented from the Bukit Batok Clubhouse. They upgraded the playground recently
Fantastic location... Dad's Corner is not to be missed.

A lovely small rainforest park tucked away in the west that feels surprisingly immersive despite its compact size. The walking trails are well-paved and shaded by tall trees, making it comfortable for both casual strolls and light workouts. There’s also a playground and a simple fitness corner, so it works well for families as well as those looking for a quick exercise session

One of the highlights is the lookout point overlooking the quarry — a peaceful spot where you can pause, enjoy the greenery, and listen to the sounds of nature. Wildlife sightings are quite common too; it’s not unusual to spot colourful birds, squirrels, and even monkeys if you’re lucky. History buffs will appreciate the remnants of WWII structures such as old pillboxes scattered around the park, which add a subtle layer of heritage to the natural scenery

The only downside at the moment is that the path leading to the Syonan Chureito (the WWII Japanese remembrance site) is under maintenance, so access is temporarily closed

It was my first and hope it's not the last visit to this place. I really want to go there again.

Really liked the place and enjoyed walking around this place. This would definitely be a cute for all of your sadness.

Didn't realise there was a quarry in the park, took a long walk in nature and found many wonderful things! Was caught by a sudden downpour, appreciated the thick trees around the area which sheltered me from the rain.

Good mix of trails, some paths are cobblestones so be in a proper footwear.

Caught a squirrel in action too!

🌳A lush greenery that is just a stone throw away from the Bukit Batok Housing Estate, providing residents living here a green lung to relax, exercise and be with nature.
🚶‍➡️🏃‍➡️Many came here in the morning for exercises and brisk walk.
🛝🏋️At the Bt Batok Nature Park, besides having numerous shelters and walking paths, there is a Children Playground for little ones to enjoy, a Foot Reflexology Path and an Adult Fitness Corner that are next to the Playground.
🏞️But the centrepiece is the Lookout Point, where one can rest, take in the sight of the tranquil water and it's scenic surrounding.
⛩️Another interesting place around the Park with World War II history is Syonan Chureito Memorial
🚧Unfortunately, the access path from the park proper is closed for safety reason and is undergoing stabilisation work which is expected to be completed end Dec 2027.
Nevertheless, the site can be reached from Lorong Sesuai.
🪨Fun fact:
Bukit Batok Nature Park was formerly a granite quarry site.
Like many other granite quarry around, it ceased operation for safety and environmental reasons. And was then transformed into a nature park for all to enjoy.
Bukit batok natural park nice and peaceful park see sunrise depend on the cloud good place exercise 2 jan 2026

Very nice place to either have a slow walk, trail walk, or trail run. There are challenging slopes & steps, but there are also friendly sections to catch your breath. Distance wise, one can choose to walk from a short 3 KM route or a longer 6KM+ route. With the terrain, it is a good place to burn some calories and at the same time, enjoy the greenery with some  sightings of animals in the wild. So far, I have encountered calugo, blue diamond coral snake, pangolin, wild boar, monitor lizards, drongo (bird) and of course, monkeys (plenty of them).

Went hiking at Bukit Timah and Rifle Range and it turned out to be a really refreshing experience. The trail was surrounded by tall trees and thick greenery, making the whole walk feel calm and peaceful. As I went down the wooden steps, I could hear the sounds of nature all around me—birds, insects, and the rustling of leaves.
Along the way, I noticed small details that made the hike even more special, like tiny plants growing out of old wood and patches of moss spreading across fallen logs. It reminded me how alive the forest is, even in the smallest places.
The hike wasn’t too easy, especially with the long paths and steps, but it felt satisfying. I managed to cover about 7.8 kilometers, and even though I was tired, it was a good kind of tired. Overall, it was a simple but meaningful experience—just being surrounded by nature, slowing down, and enjoying the moment.

The highest hill in Sg but there is no view at the summit. A little disappointing. Very hot and humid. The forest does not cover or cool down the trail unlike the one at Macritchi Reservoir. Pavillion are not powered with fans and have no vending machines. Prefer the treetop trail at Macritchi reservoir.

Bukit Timah Nature Reserve is a great spot to switch into hiking mode on the weekend.

There are lots of stairs, so be ready for a workout — definitely a sweat-it-out session 💦
Perfect if you’re looking to move your body and get a good sweat in nature.
Brisk walking during the early morning... able to feel the fresh air... a good place to have a short exercises as well.

Located a short walk from Bukit Gombak MRT, this peaceful park is perfect for a brief escape into nature. Its compact trail takes less than 20 minutes to explore, offering serene greenery and a calming atmosphere. Ideal for a quick, refreshing break from the city’s hustle!

Convenient parking area within the park but pretty limited. Otherwise may park at multi storey carpark next to it as just short walking distance.
New park with little she'd but still nice to walk during evening, not so crowded.
Suitable for families with kids.
Half way up, there is a small farm where people planting varies vegetables.
Kids love it to find out how the plants grow.
There is also a small dog run area too, kids love to watch different dogs running around.

It is a decent park in Bukit Gombak and is a bit small. There are only one toilet in the park due to its small area and there is a beautiful butterfly garden here. Remember to bring a umbrella here as it might get quite hot as there are several flights of stairs in the park due to the elevation of the hill. Be prepared to climb some flights of stairs in the park and enjoy yourself in the beautiful scenery around you. Overall a decent park near a residential area.

Clean, quiet and nice fresh view of greenery with a small dog play area, basketball court, exercise corner and 2 small playgrounds.
My kids love it. There is also a cafe there "Whisk & Paddle" and I heard that the price for a cafe is somewhat overpriced.
Will review it during my visit there...

Good place for simple exercise as the highest point is up a small hill. There is a dog run as well but gets crowded on weekends. There is a butterfly park but not many butterflies spotted. perhaps need to go there in the morning. There is a cafe called Whisk and Paddle at the entrance of the park.

After 20 years living in Singapore 🇸🇬, finally my first time visiting Little Guilin 🌿⛰️

Honestly such a peaceful hidden gem in the middle of the city. Came around 5PM and the weather was still quite hot ☀️🥵, but once reaching around 6PM, the whole atmosphere changed completely 🍃✨ The breeze became so cooling and relaxing 😌

The beautiful lake view with the huge granite rocks really gave me a different feeling. A perfect place to slow down from busy city life 💚

Good place for:
✨ Relaxing
✨ Chill evening walk
✨ Meditation 🧘‍♀️
✨ Clear your mind
✨ Hang out with friends & family

Sometimes we keep searching for faraway places, but Singapore still has many simple beautiful spots waiting to be explored 🇸🇬❤️

Definitely a place worth visiting at least once, especially during sunset 🌅

#LittleGuilin #BukitBatok #SingaporeHiddenGem #RelaxingPlace #NatureHealing #EveningChill #MeditationSpot #SingaporeLife #PeacefulVibes #ExploreSingapore

Little Guilin

Finally made it here. Took the MRT to Bukit Gombak, walked past the hilly stadium area, and eventually reached the river where the sign for Little Guilin appears.

It’s a lovely spot for a relaxed morning—perfect for a light walk after a workout, followed by a simple picnic or just sitting quietly and daydreaming. The park isn’t very large, but that’s part of its charm. A peaceful little place for a quick moment of healing and calm.
Posting photos taken during Covid years. Frequently visited this place to keep my sanity.  Thankful. A nice place to bask in nature and have some time to yourself. Beautiful water reflection.

29MAR2026
We came here just to see how the place was. It was a disappointment as we expected it to be great. However the water was green so the overall view was not that great. But still, this is Singapore not China😊😊😊😊😊😊no complaints 😊😊😊😊😊

This quarry in Bukit Batok / Bukit Gombak (?) used to be a go-to setting for shooting "wuxia" scenes for local TV shows in the 1980s and maybe for some time after as well - since it gave to some extent the impression of the scenes being shot in China or particularly, Guilin; hence its moniker, "Little Guilin." This quarry is a nice place to walk around with its calm waters and picturesque rocky outcrop set amidst  walking trails with trees and greeneries abound - making it a pleasant place to unwind and to get close to nature.

Visited a friend and i don't see crack tiles everywhere. I understand that their shuttle bus is permanently servicing the residence to clementi which is a must for this area considering the walk in from clementi is quite far. However, they have a bus stop on along AYE which is just 4-5mins walk. I would say it is not as inaccessible as i first thought it was....
Is a very well- kept condo ...suitable for small family as the floor area not very big. But its environment is nice ...got a very well built roof garden :)
Met an old friend here. The place looks nice. We lost in touch soon after. Pity

It is a cosy place to stay. Ambience of a resort. Without telling you more, you will be thinking as if you are staying in resorts such as  Phuket, Bali, Pattaya or even Seychelles. Reasonable size function room with nice blueish swimming pool coupled with nice beautiful clouds. The units of each apartments ceiling is about 3m high. Behind it has a big n nice public park. Built n designed by World Class but minimum it a class of its uniqueness. Hard to come by with nice and big landscape. Nowadays it is difficult to find a condominiums with such a big and scenic landscape.
viewed two units. location not bad, but the workmanship is terrible. it is ridiculous that a 5 years condo has peeling tiles everywhere and broken sewage filter here and there.
peaceful, shaddy, breezy walk. what I like about walking here it is uncluttered, literally having the wholevplace to myself and tgere is plentify of trees and nice scenery.

✨𝗛𝗶𝗴𝗵𝗹𝘆 𝗿𝗲𝗰𝗼𝗺𝗺𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗱✨

🩵🩷I enrolled my daughter at Small Wonder (159 Bukit Batok), and I couldn’t be happier. The teachers and staff are incredibly caring, observant, and professional. Principal Ivy is fantastic—always around to ensure everything runs smoothly.🧡💜

✏️🪑The classrooms are spacious, clean, and well-equipped with engaging learning materials. My daughter loves singing, reading, going on morning walks, and playing both at the playground and in school! but most importantly, it’s the teachers who truly bring everything to life. That’s why I trust Small Wonder (159 Bukit batok) —because the teachers are kind, caring, and deeply invested in the children’s growth.💞🌱

✨🍊I used to worry about my daughter’s Mandarin since we don’t speak it at home, but the bilingual curriculum (English-Mandarin) has been great! She’s picked it up so well and can now communicate in Mandarin comfortably—I’m beyond happy with her progress!

I also appreciate the parent app—it keeps us updated with photos, lesson plans, and important notices, and even allows direct chats with teachers. Their regular updates make me feel so involved in my daughter’s preschool journey.🍎👍

🌈I was so impressed that I even recommended my nephew to join—and he did! Huge thanks to the amazing team. Keep up the great work! 🌈

Teachers at this outlet are always so kind and caring, and full of energy. They check on our kids and gave us updates regularly. They also helped my son get used to the school very quickly and he loves the teachers there. I can see my son learning many things and becoming more and more independant, he also learnt to express himself more at home. Overall thumbs up!

One of the better preschools in the area. My kid was enrolled in PN early this year and was under the care of Teacher Xinru. She is a fantastic teacher, and is always giving updates and is easy to reach. Only exposure I have of other teachers are during checkin/out and the occassional field trips. They seem equally fantastic and capable. Fees-wise, it is affordable and really value for money. I feel safe and assured leaving my kid here while I work.

I enrolled my children at Small Wonder @ Bukit Batok in the Nursery class under the care of Teacher Syah and ShuQi LaoShi, and I couldn’t be happier with our experience. Both teachers are truly fantastic. My kids genuinely look forward to going to school every day, which says so much as a parent.

All the teachers here are very loving and attentive, showing great care and patience towards every child. They greet the children with big smiles every morning and are always energetic and positive, creating a warm and welcoming environment. The teachers regularly update us on our children’s daily school routine, including meals, naps, and potty progress. Whenever we message them with questions, they respond promptly and handle matters efficiently, which gives us great peace of mind.

What really impressed me is how much improvement I can see in my children in just one month, both in their behaviour and overall development. I’m truly grateful to the teachers and the school for their dedication and care. Highly recommended to parents looking for a nurturing and supportive preschool environment.

We enrolled our son at Small Wonder BB at the start of the year. We’ve been really happy with the teachers at SW BB. They’re so patient, caring, and encouraging, and it really shows in how the kids grow and enjoy school. Our little one always goes to school and comes home happy, which is reassuring to see. Big thanks to the team for creating such a supportive and fun environment. We’d definitely recommend SW BB.

place_name: UOB Branch - Bukit Batok Central Branch
review_text:
For many many many years, I have been telling the teller staff of some sg banks why so much of paper works is needed to process a basic renewal of fixed deposit.

But to my surprise today at Bukit Batok UOB branch, it took me less than 6-10mins to renew my FD and to withdraw the interest cash out after my Q number was called.

Well done. Hope all other banks can do.

place_name: UOB Branch - Bukit Batok Central Branch
review_text: I have visited the UOB Bukit Batok branch many times, and Julie Fam has assisted me on several occasions. Each time, she has been consistently professional, efficient, and knowledgeable at the counter. She handles transactions accurately and promptly, while always communicating with a warm and friendly smile. Her excellent customer service leaves a very positive impression and truly represents the high standards of the bank. A consistently pleasant experience—well deserved 5 stars!

place_name: UOB Branch - Bukit Batok Central Branch
review_text:
Shout out to Elaine from UOB for being so kind and helpful. My mom and I had some enquiries regarding credit cards and loans, and she patiently guided us step by step while helping us convey our situation clearly to the relevant departments.

We truly appreciate her patience, attentiveness, and the time she took to assist us.

place_name: UOB Branch - Bukit Batok Central Branch
review_text:
I went to bukit batok central branch for opening a shared account.. i was served by a nice friendly Mr Darren Wee (branch manager). He was so patience in explaining and answering all our enquiry.. It was done smoothly together with the help of his colleague Maam Dorothy Tang too..
Mr Darren even gave birthday wishes card to my mum since its coming in few days time.. It means alot to put a smile on my mum face.. Thankyou so much for the pleasant experience..

place_name: UOB Branch - Bukit Batok Central Branch
review_text: Big shout out to Jason Chia for his excellent service, going above and beyond even staying back after hours to help. Overall the staff at Bukit Batok branch is very professional and friendly. Highly recommended.

place_name: Mind Stretcher Learning Centre (Bukit Batok Central)
review_text: I am a student in the graduating class of grade six I am doing english, and I would like to thank Ms Michelle Ko as she has truly helped me a lot with my vocabulary, grammar and comprehension writing, and she takes the time to explain certain words and vocabulary so that we can better understand the content in the work booklet. Some things I live about her is that once you open up to her she is truly a very nice person and she always makes her classes fun, with her entertaining stories and personality. I am truly lucky to have had her as my teacher for the last few years. I will miss you!!

place_name: Mind Stretcher Learning Centre (Bukit Batok Central)
review_text: Happy Teacher’s Day! Thank you for guiding my child with such care, patience, and dedication. We truly appreciate the love and effort you put into teaching and helping them grow.

place_name: Mind Stretcher Learning Centre (Bukit Batok Central)
review_text:
Hello, this is Krish from P6 Science on Monday from 7-9 p.m.

Learning from Mr Affendy has been an amazing experience. I always try to give my best in my work and understand the topics, but whenever something is unclear, he goes out of his way to explain it. Whether it’s through drawings, hand gestures, or creative examples, he always makes sure that the class truly understands.

Even when I lose focus and he reminds me to pay attention, I know it is for my own good. Thanks to his patience and guidance, my grades have improved so much! From struggling to even reach 70, I now score above 90 regularly. It’s not just me either! I believe the whole class has benefitted from his teaching.

I also enjoy his jokes, which give us a little laugh-break to relax before getting back to work with even more focus. Mr Affendy makes learning both effective and enjoyable, and that makes a big difference.

I want to thank him for going the extra mile for us, and for always believing in his students.

Happy Teacher’s Day, Mr Affendy!

With gratitude,
Krish

place_name: Mind Stretcher Learning Centre (Bukit Batok Central)
review_text:
Thank you, Mr Affendi, for being such a “bright spark” in my learning journey! You’ve made science exciting, and I’m truly grateful for all your guidance. Wishing you a Happy Teacher’s Day, with lots of positive energy!

S1 Science - Ayden Lee KZ

place_name: Mind Stretcher Learning Centre (Bukit Batok Central)
review_text:
