place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (Bukit Batok)
review_text:
We are very happy with the progress our son has made at this Chinese tuition centre. Since enrolling, we can clearly see improvements in his Chinese — he is able to recognise more words and his pronunciation has become much clearer. This has given him more confidence, especially now that he is in P6.
We also really appreciate the teachers’ commitment to keeping us updated on his progress in class. They regularly share feedback on the areas he needs to work on, which helps us support and guide him better at home. The communication has been very helpful and reassuring as parents.
Overall, we are grateful for the dedicated teachers and their patient, structured approach to teaching. We would definitely recommend this centre to parents who are looking for effective Chinese tuition and attentive educators

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (Bukit Batok)
review_text:
Enrolled my 4-year-old in this Chinese enrichment class, it has been over a year and it’s been a really positive experience so far. The teachers are patient, dedicated, and genuinely caring — they make the lessons engaging and age-appropriate, which helps the kids stay interested and enjoy learning.

My son loves coming for Teacher Liu class.
I’ve noticed my child becoming more confident with the language, and that’s something I truly appreciate. Grateful to have found a team of teachers who put in so much heart into what they do.

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (Bukit Batok)
review_text:
My daughter is currently in Secondary 3 and has been attending Higher Chinese classes here. We are very satisfied with the learning program and teaching approach. The teachers are patient, dedicated, and able to explain complex topics in a clear and engaging way. The lessons are well-structured, and the materials provided are very helpful for exam preparation.

Most importantly, my daughter enjoys the classes and feels motivated to do better. I would highly recommend this tuition centre to any student looking to improve their Higher Chinese.

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (Bukit Batok)
review_text:
We’ve been very happy with our experience at the centre so far. Our child is currently attending P5 lessons, and his feedback has been postitive. The teacher is patient, attentive, and explains concepts in a way that is both clear and effective.

Lessons are well-structured, with a strong focus not only on understanding the topic but also on answering techniques. This has helped our child keep up without feeling overwhelmed.

We also really appreciate the regular updates after each lesson, which keep us informed and provide reassurance on our child’s progress.

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (Bukit Batok)
review_text: My daughter began her tuition here a few months ago, and I have observed improvement in her performance. She has also become more confident and motivated in her Chinese language. Her teacher has been exceptionally patient and supportive, especially in helping her overcome her initial challenges. I highly recommend this centre

A rather run down coffeeshop that houses my favourite HK Roast stall. Most of the stalls in this coffeeshop came & gone, even some good ones. However, the HK Roast & Halal Chicken Rice seems to stand tall.
Outstanding roasted pork and char siew, note that they only accept cash and that seating can be limited during peak hours. But well worth the trouble 👍🏻

Overall, a nice place located in the Bukit Batok Neighborhood. The most famous stall is definitely the chicken rice stall. The snacks, drinks and rojak, and Sedap Corner are also the oldest stores. The variety of food choices is quite limited, and many stalls are empty for lease. The Food Centre is quite clean, but the seats are not really enough if you come during peak hours thus finding a seat can be an issue. Overall a nice place with good food to try.

Reviewing on the noodle stall in the S-11 coffee shop.
Ordered the laksa for lunch. Did not enough the meal. Sufficient quantity of ingredients for the price. However, the whole bowl of noodle was way too oily. The taste wasn’t good and definitely unhealthy to be eating food with so much oil. Also I felt that the noodles wasn’t cooked properly.
Sad to say, not a pleasant experience.

$18 for a bowl of mala! Rediculous.. Ive eaten mala from soany places and i can clearly say my portion is just $10+.. Taste quite bad.. No mala flavour also. Definitely not going back again.. Will not recommend to anyone. Rather go to kopitiam westmall for better mala

Updated food vendors since Mei December took over a few years ago. Many more food variety with the usual economy rice, hainanese chicken rice, to Yung tow fu, coffee stall as well as a nonya chendol and Chinese pancake stalls. There is something for everyone here. Place is brightly lighted and well maintained. Very clean.  Very busy especially during bfast and lunch hour

First visit and highly recommended by my sister in law. Newly renovated. Toilet modern and clean. Let's hope the public will use it responsibly.

This coffee shop is huge with many stalls. Good selection and good food.

The contractor engaged to cleans the tables do a good job. Unlike other hawker centres where the cleaners simply sweep the leftover food stuff on to the floor and do not clean the tables.

I hope the supervisor continue to motivate the staff to do well. Afterall, this is our country and we should be proud to keep everything and everywhere clean.

Best mee siam! Got to try this! Big portion, delicious and value for money at $3.50! Takeaway container impressive with separate noodle and soup portion. They also do party orders but you have to provide the containers. Try the curry puff next to it too!

I have never ate at a kpt while getting attacked by ants. Damn gaowei leh 😭 Other kpts have trees nearby also but I don’t get attacked by ants. My whole meal, i caught like 10 ants on my body & 5 on my bag 😭 Can the management look into this!! Pls!! I want to eat my meal in peace!!

Today, I was invited to meet up for lunch by @june_shijie at @meide_coffeeshop 美德咖啡店, at Bukit Batok.

Even before reaching, the name already intrigued me as 美德 meant virtue, so I definitely have to give it a go.

Hopefully, I am virtuous enough to eat there and hopefully, the coffeeshop lives up to its name.

Indeed, I wasn't disappointed.

Mei De has all the good food from renowned stalls, and there are so many stalls. It's like visiting those food streets.

In addition, it comprises two coffeeshops face to face, so you get two times the options of food and drinks. In fact, you can literally try different stalls there daily, over an entire month.

Today, I tried the @lixinfishball Li Xin Teochew Fishball Noodles 立兴潮州鱼圆面, which has a Michelin Gourmand Bib in  2022.

The fishball and fishcakes were extremely fresh and bouncy, while the meepok was very Q and tasty. Their chilli and sauce were good, not overtly spicy.

The iced lemon tea there was very rich as well.

Thanks for the invitation, June. I will definitely be looking forward to try other things there.

place_name: NAN YANG DAO 南洋岛 @West Mall
review_text:
Utterly disappointing experience!!!

I visited Nanyang Dao at around 9:15pm, well before their last order timing of 9:30pm. Since they were still accepting customers, we were seated and made our orders quickly out of courtesy.

What followed was a completely unacceptable experience.

We ordered a fried kuey teow and fried pork belly rice bowl for dine-in, and later added a fried chicken for takeaway. I personally informed the cashier/manager that the fried chicken was takeaway.

The fried kuey teow arrived first. The rice bowl took a long time, and when it finally came, it was served in a takeaway container. After I clarified it was for dine-in, the staff gave an audible sigh and showed clear displeasure.

Worse still, the food was simply dumped out from the takeaway box onto a plate and served back to me. The bowl was oily, presentation was sloppy, and the dish looked completely unappetising. This is unacceptable for an establishment that charges service charge and GST. Even hawker stalls that doesn’t charge service charge and GST have more pride in their food.

The fried chicken, which I clearly stated was takeaway, was later served on a dine-in plate showing a complete lack of communication within the team.

When I raised these issues, the kitchen staff had the audacity to accuse me of “changing my order,” which is completely untrue. I had already clearly placed my order via the app and personally informed them of the takeaway item. Instead of taking responsibility for their mistake, they chose to shift the blame onto the customer.

The attitude throughout was defensive, dismissive, and unprofessional. There was no accountability, no proper apology, and no effort to rectify the situation.

I eventually asked to cancel the rice dish and packed the fried chicken myself, as I had lost confidence in how my food was being handled.

Being close to closing time is not an excuse for poor service, careless food handling, or blaming customers for staff mistakes. If you are not willing to serve properly, then stop accepting customers before closing.

Very disappointing experience.

place_name: NAN YANG DAO 南洋岛 @West Mall
review_text: .: : :::  - 南洋岛 @ West Mall has a bright, comfy dine-in space and the food arrives quickly. The noodles and fried rice were satisfying, with generous portions and punchy, savory flavors. However, the physical menu is outdated—quite a few listed items aren’t actually available. To avoid disappointment, refer to the online menu before ordering and double-check with staff. Overall, a decent casual spot, but the menu mismatch hurts the experience. Still worth a visit though.

place_name: NAN YANG DAO 南洋岛 @West Mall
review_text:
Tried the Nanyang Nasi Lemak at NAN YANG DAO in West Mall and really enjoyed it. The portion was generous and surprisingly filling, with fragrant rice, crispy ikan bilis, peanuts, sambal, and a nicely done egg coming together well. The flavors felt authentic and comforting, very much in the style of classic Malaysian cafe food

To me, the nasi lemak is definitely comparable to Oriental Kopi, which says a lot given how popular Oriental Kopi has become. The restaurant itself also has a pleasant and modern kopitiam-style atmosphere that makes it comfortable for both quick meals and casual dining

place_name: NAN YANG DAO 南洋岛 @West Mall
review_text:
Disappointing chendol. The ice was packed so densely that it was impossible to mix properly, and it felt more like a frozen block taken from a freezer than freshly shaved ice. There was very little coconut milk and gula melaka flavor, I could only taste ice.

When I mentioned this to the cashier, I was told that the person who made the chendol was new. However, the issue isn’t whether the staff member is new, the problem is that the ice was not prepared like proper freshly shaved ice.

At $3.50 per bowl, I expected much better. I ordered two bowls but couldn’t even finish one because it was mostly hard, tasteless ice. The toppings were decent, but the dessert lacked the rich, creamy, and fragrant flavors that a good chendol should have.

What was disappointing was that the response was simply an apology, no compensation was offered, and I was not informed that any feedback would be passed on to management. It was also especially disappointing as this was advertised as a “best seller” item. Overall, very disappointing for the price paid.

place_name: NAN YANG DAO 南洋岛 @West Mall
review_text: The fried chicken mee Siam  is too dry. I like the Penang 粿条, spicy enough and tasty. The 蛤蜊soup is tasty also. The coffee C is too bitter and not creamy. Overall, food is average. The service is good, the aunty is polite, attentive and fast.

I would like to compliment Mdm Ho Geok Huay from Sheng Siong for her excellent service and kindness at the payment counter.

While I was scanning my purchases at the self-checkout counter, Mdm Ho Geok Huay noticed that I was managing the items on my own and voluntarily stepped forward to assist me. Even though customers are usually expected to scan and pack their own purchases, she took the initiative to help scan my items and carefully packed everything neatly into my own bag.

What touched me most was that she offered her help without being asked. Her willingness to go the extra mile showed genuine care, attentiveness, and dedication towards customers. Her friendly and helpful attitude made my shopping experience much smoother and more pleasant.

In today’s busy environment, it is truly heartwarming to meet a staff member like Mdm Ho Geok Huay who serves customers with such sincerity and initiative. Sheng Siong is fortunate to have an employee like her, and I hope her excellent service and positive attitude will be recognized and appreciated.

I would also like to share some feedback regarding the Sheng Siong outlet at 292 Bukit Batok. As there are many residents staying around this area, the outlet tends to be quite crowded and feels relatively small compared to many other Sheng Siong branches. I hope the management may consider expanding the store in the future or increasing the variety of products available. In particular, the household products section is currently quite limited. I believe an expanded range of products and more space would greatly benefit the residents in the surrounding neighbourhood and further improve the shopping experience for customers.
great soermarket. my favorite go-to place for Chinese cooking ingredients. very comprehensive range of veggies, meat and seafood.

I went to the Sheng Siong at Bukit Batok West Shopping Centre in the morning but didn’t get anything as it was packed with customers. This outlet is much better for customers to shop leisurely, as it’s less crowded. I managed to buy what I wanted in less than half an hour.
Sheng Siong is great for cheap groceries. Prices are lower than most places, and fresh items are decent. Stores can be crowded, but good value for everyday shopping.
Maybe this is the smallest sheng siong that I have been to. Very busy and narrow so hard to shop. Well stocked items and staff are friendly. There is a multi story car park

I had an unpleasant experience at 7‑Eleven. One of the young female staff members displayed a very poor attitude. She was not friendly and seemed irritated when I asked simple questions, responding in an unapproachable and sulky manner.
Customer service is important, especially in a convenience store setting, and I hope the management can provide proper training to improve staff professionalism and communication.

18 Sep 25 at 3:59am
A male employee was busy occupying himself with his game on his phone, did not even bother to ask for my yuu member. When I showed my yuu member, he cancelled the whole transaction, and rescanned my items. Every move he made, from tapping hard on the POS to pressing the cancel button on the payment terminal to throwing the utensil on the table, this guy seriously has a problem, big problem.

Once the transaction completed, he did not even bother to say thank you, tore the receipt and got back to his stupid game.

I hope, karma hits him bad, real bad.
The store manager Cassie was very helpful and was willing to process a prepaid SIM card application even though it is rush hour on a Friday and getting crowded. Much appreciated.

29 January 2025 Sunday at 9.11pm. I went to purchase a Viceroy Double Melo cigarette priced $15.80 at the counter and a young male cashier with a gold earring on the left of his ear took my order. I paid by pay wave using my iPhone and the first transaction failed so he demanded that I made another payment. The second transaction was successful and I walked away thinking that it was normal. However, when I reached home, I checked my bank account for the transaction and the purchase was deducted twice. I felt shocked and thinking that I was cheated. Does this happened to any other customer too? Hope the next customer won’t fall victim for this unfair actions by the counter staff when making cardless payment. Can the management look on this review and check for any other similar cases?

On 5 September 2025, at about 835pm - 845pm, went to the store to buy a roblox robux card for my son. But the guy put back the card and simply say the system is maxed out and cannot buy it anymore for the day. Asked me to come back tomorrow. I went to other 7-11s around bukit batok  area and ask the cashiers about this and they said they have never heard about this kind of system before. To the guy who dont want to sell me the roblox card, if you dont want to work please give the job to other people who need it more.

I bought an ice cup for 1.50 and I didn’t know it was cracked until I poured my drink inside. Luckily I was right outside the store and I quickly applied for a new one or a refund. However, the Indian lady cashier rejected me, stating that the ice cup was fine when she scanned it. She insisted it and I couldn’t do anything else.
Today I go top up my ezlink card then I said $5 but they put 50 the staff name is priya and and she did not pay me back my money and only gave me 22 cents!!!!!!?!? Like that's so rude

cashier was very rude the food I bought was $4.20 she asked me for 20¢ coins which I didn't have so I told her I don't have coins as I js cleared my coins and she proceeded to snatch my wallet from me to check if I really didn't have coins

A very convenient location for the people around. Just go under the block and get whatever is needed. Mainly for those who feel hungry at mid night. This 7-eleven has high chairs for customers to have meal, which makes this store unique compare to other nearby 7-eleven stores.
Came at 12 midnight, no staff around. Wait outside for 15 mins still no staff come in.

Since childhood, this place has been standing tall for over 30 years.

Question is, why is it unlike other markets in the west? Housewives will probably tell you that there are more variety of fishes and “sea creatures” sold here compared to everywhere else. 😄

It is true, to some extend because I was still able to find Spanish Mackerel or also known as Tenggiri / Batang , at 1pm ! If it was Bukit Panjang Market, you will never find it by 10-11 am since it is quite a popular type of fish.

Wanted to look for the elusive Toli Shad also known as Terubuk but did not manage to since I was in a hurry.

Nevertheless, Gombak Market is like a “vintage watch haven”. Sorry if the comparison is way put of base here. 😂

Come on down and see for yourself what this interesting Wet Market has to offer. Not forgetting the wide selection of breakfast available at its many coffeeshops under one roof.

Bukit Batok Town Park is a small but pleasant park located right beside Little Guilin (Guilin Quarry). While the park itself isn’t very large, it offers some nice walking paths, shaded areas, and viewpoints of the quarry lake and rocky cliffs

The highlight is definitely the scenic view of the quarry, where the dramatic rock formations reflect beautifully on the water. There are also a few quiet spots to sit and relax, including sheltered benches and shaded trails that make for a comfortable stroll

Despite encountering dirty water upon arrival, this spot still offers a pleasant retreat for chilling out or going for a run. It's perfect for a laid-back picnic with friends or family by the lake, surrounded by nature's beauty.

⛰️Bukit Batok Town Park is also known as Little Guilin 小桂林 because of the majestic rock formation on the tranquil lake that resemble the actual Guilin in China's Guangxi Province.
🏞️Surrounding the tranquil lake waters are expansive lush vegetations and matured tall trees which provide a natural ambience and shady enviroment.
⛰️Little Guilin lies just across the Bukit Batok residential estate.
⛰️It's offers a convenient and serene retreat for the residents living nearby.
⛰️The Town Park is easily accessible by public transport. It is right at Bukit Gombak MRT Station.
📤Bukit Gombak Trail:
📤Bukit Batok Town Park used to have a Bukit Gombak Trail. The nature trail started from Bukit Gombak Stadium and went across Gombak ridge towards Hillview. It ended at Chu Lin Rd.
📤But a landslide occurred along the trail at Chu Lin Road end; and for safety reason, the nature trail was closed off to public access in 2007.
🏞️Fun fact:
⛰️Bukit Gombak and other hills around it, like Bukit Batok and Bukit Timah, used to be a vibrant granite quarry area during its heyday.
🏞️When the granite quarry industries ceased their operations, the abandoned quarries soon became the city's natural landscape and nature parks.
🏞️Little Guilin was the former site of Gammon Quarry which ceased operation in 1984.
It was converted into Bukit Batok Town Park a year later.
⛰️Bukit Batok, which means "coughing hill" was derived from the sound of constant blasting of the quarry activities.

I visited Bukit Batok Town Park yesterday around noon, and I was amazed by the breathtaking beauty of this place. With its serene lake and magnificent rock formation, also known as Little Guilin or Xiao Guilin, the park offers a peaceful escape from the bustling city.

However, it’s important to be cautious about the monkeys that inhabit the area, so remember to keep a close eye on your bags and belongings.

The calm atmosphere and picturesque surroundings make it a perfect spot for gatherings and day-time parties with friends. Let’s all do our part to preserve the cleanliness of this place and ensure its enjoyment for everyone.
A tranquil, scenic gem—perfect for a brief nature retreat and striking photos. Ideal for quick escapes; combine with nearby trails for a longer adventure.

Visited in March 2024.

The abandoned park is fairly dangerous in certain areas, particularly the famed Torii boardwalk, which is a great way to cross from the human world to the land of the kami in a permanent way with a wrong step. Fallen trees have crushed the boardwalk and obstructed it at the same time, so a visitor cannot go to the other end of the park without exiting or navigating the steep slopes. I also saw bees as I was coming out, 20 or so, on an artificial rock near the stone step entrance.

The abandoned kampong plantation behind the Hillside park at BB West Ave 5 is much more interesting in my opinion. There was the ruins of what likely was the main house with a dried up well, a big circular open water tank and another rectangular water tank. The 2 water tanks were located behind the house where a small stream, likely a former longkang, supplied water to the circular water tank. This circular tank had 1 or 2 catfish inside, which may explain the clear water and relative lack of mosquitoes. Behind the circular tank is a rectangular water tank that likely used to rear fish, but has since cracked after being abandoned. It is very tranquil there, and is certainly worth a short visit, as it is not too difficult to reach.

Do be aware of wild boars and snakes, I was alerted by a pair of Bangladeshi workers that a snake dropped in front of me, which I failed to notice. They also mentioned seeing wild boars and their digging activities, so watch out. Oh, also got pandan growing there.

Both Hillside Park 1 and 2 are essential climate control mechanism which are highly beneficial to the health of residents within a 1km radius from its location. We must protect Hill 2 from being exploited by HDB (which it did to Hill 1). Hill 2 actually lowers the ambient temperature by up to 3 deg C across the Bukit Batok Polyclinic.

Nestled amidst lush greenery, the church offers a quiet space for reflection. Whether you’re a Catholic or someone seeking spiritual inspiration, St. Mary of the Angels is a must-visit for its architecture, atmosphere, and welcoming spirit.

Prawns are fresh ,  pork belly is savoury, sotong is well cooked, pork lard is crispy and fragrant , chili is spicy and aromatic, portion is worth the $6

Only thing lacking is it’s a tad too salty and also I don’t really understand the need of charging for extra chili. Can you maintain the same amount of chili given for all orders? If not there are bound to  be unequal amount every order so I don’t see why one can’t ask for a little more when they feel the chili is lacking. Usually I don’t ask for more chili but it still puts me off when I see stalls with such notices.

After Tan Song Heng Fried Hokkien Mee at Commonwealth hawker centre closed down for retirement, I went on an island-wide hunt for good Hokkien Mee. Then I found this place! This is by far the best fried Hokkien Mee in my opinion! There is ample "wok hey", the stock is full of flavour, not too thick and not too sweet. At $6 a plate, you get 2 medium size prawns. $8 plate comes with 4 medium size prawn. $10 plate gets 4 large prawns. This stall is now my go to place for fried Hokkien Mee every other week LOL.

The hokkien mee here is a luscious, “wet” style plate where the yellow mee and thick bee hoon have basically held a private meeting with a high-pressure wok. The prawns are snappy, the lard is crispy, and the squid is tender enough to make you emotional.

I’ve posted more photos and some tips on visiting this stall on my Instagram @iamgettingfat. Do check it out :)

It’s been a while since we’ve wanted to try this Hokkien Mee, and we’ve heard good reviews about it.

Finally, we made time to go with a group of friends and give it a try.
We initially wanted to order five plates of Hokkien Mee, but the shop assistant suggested the sharing portion for 6–7 people at $35.
When the huge plate of Hokkien Mee arrived, it felt like we were in a food challenge like Zermatt Neo 😂

The pork lard was crispy and very tasty!
The noodles had a nice wok hei, well done, but lacked a bit of umami flavor.

Overall, it’s good! But I’d probably only crave it when I’m around that area.

First of all, thank you Shaw for taking over Cathay and giving us West-siders a proper cinema again! Super grateful to have a nice place nearby to catch movies. I want to commend the team for transforming the space into a much more comfortable and movie-vibe environment compared to the previous operator (specifically the hallway and toilets). Took many photos during my trip.

I recently had the opportunity to experience the Premiere halls (specifically Premiere 1 & Premiere 2). I understand that this is a new team and they are still familiarising themselves with the SOPs. I can see they are trying their best. Still, I would like to share both the positive aspects and areas for improvement based on my visits.

What I Loved:
1) I tried the seafood pizza & it was delicious. Although the price is on the higher side, the quality justifies it. The food definitely stands out as one of the highlights of the Premiere experience.

2) The adjustable sofa chairs are very comfortable & the overall privacy in both Premiere halls is excellent. It really elevates the experience and makes the upgrade feel worthwhile.

3) When I caught The Predator at Premiere 2 hall, the sound was loud, immersive & clear. Exactly what I expect from a premium hall. Sadly wasn’t the case for Premiere 1 hall.

Areas of Improvement:
1) The lighting for the seat numbers is very bright in Premiere 1 hall (refer to photo) and was distracting during the movie. Dimming them slightly would improve the overall ambience.

2) Because the team is new, I understand they’re still adapting but there were noticeable inconsistencies:
- Premiere 2 hall, I was given cutlery and tissue with my pizza. Premiere 1 hall, I wasn’t.
- I encountered issues with both Premiere halls: once I couldn’t use the discount code and another time my order didn’t arrive until staff repeatedly came back and forth to resolve it.
The staff are young and clearly trying their best but the service processes may need refinement so the experience feels smooth and premium.

3) When I watched Wicked in Premiere 1, the audio sounded muffled and soft, especially from the front speakers. This was quite noticeable and very different from my excellent experience in Premiere 2. I’m not sure if this was due to the sound mix provided for the film or the hall setup but the inconsistency between the two halls was obvious.

Despite the hiccups, the improvements to the cinema are significant & I appreciate having such a comfortable well-designed place close to home. Just need adjustments to service consistency and technical setup (JEM please don’t disappoint Shaw good sound system reputation).

Thank you.

This Fuji Hill Park, lies in Bukit Batok Ave 3, is indeed a big nice serene park, allowing one to do brisk walking, exercising or having a simple meal at sheltered areas with friends, in the morning or evening is truly relaxing.
To chat and walk along the upward, downward greenery hill slopes, the long wooden bridge, snap and video some flowers and scene along the path bring meaning to the trail.
Children too discover the fun challenges while playing around the few colourful playgrounds, especially with their parents around, watching and encouraging them while they explore on.
Indeed this neighborhood park is a wonderful park to linger and relax, especially on a cool breezy day.

“Fuji Hill Park” is an informal name referring to the small hill within Bukit Batok Neighbourhood Park.
It’s a pleasant little green space located not far from Bukit Batok Central ~ perfect for a leisurely stroll, getting some fresh air, or a bit of light exercise.

The climb up the hill provided a light work out for the children. Once we were at the top, the children spied the playgrounds.

They then began speeding down the board walk towards the tall slides.

The play area consisted of 4 different playgrounds. The large play structure consisted of a long slide which ran along the slope of the hill. Looks to be quite fun as the children could play here for quite some time. However this area was a bit dimly lit at the middle sections so one should consider going in the evenings before dark.

Overall a nice pleasant park on the hill with an interesting play area.

This is certainly one of the most thrilling slide in Singapore! The playground and slide start on the top side of the hill; and the slide ends further down the hill. The gradient is also steep with the slide tube interlaced with clear slide tubes. This gives the most sensational feeling of free falling!!

A wonderful slide indeed, and suitable for 8 to 12 year olds.
Excellent playground. A walk to the summit was a good exercise, but the view was not really worth the effort. There are 3 playgrounds that caters to children of all ages.

place_name: Bukit Batok Nature Park Lookout Point
review_text:
Bukit Batok Nature Park offers a peaceful escape with beautiful views, though it doesn't have one officially named "Lookout Point." Instead, you'll find numerous elevated trails and spots that provide lovely perspectives.

As you explore, you'll be treated to panoramic greenery, with dense trees and glimpses of the serene quarry lake below. On clear days, you might even catch distant views of the city, creating a unique contrast between nature and urban life.

These higher areas are perfect for quiet contemplation, photography, and enjoying fresh air. The park's paths are well-maintained, making these scenic spots accessible.

SUSS has been a meaningful part of my academic journey, especially since it was the only institution that accepted my application. While the curriculum is undeniably rigorous, it has also pushed me to grow intellectually and adapt to increasingly complex expectations, particularly as I approach Year 3. Some assignments are quite challenging and require a high level of critical thinking, which can be demanding but ultimately rewarding.

One concern, however, is the school’s efforts to combat the use of AI. While the intention is understandable, the system has flagged a significant number of false positives, creating unnecessary stress and anxiety among students. This has, at times, affected the overall learning experience and assignments submission. I hope that the institution can fine tune and resolve this issue soon.

Additionally, certain components such as the e-portfolio—especially in the last semester—feel like an added chores for students. Instead of adding meaningful value to learning, it can come across as repetitive and disconnected from more practical or relevant academic outcomes. Suss might want to review if Eportfolio is truly necessary.

Despite these challenges, SUSS remains a good institution that offers a strong platform for preparing students for the realities of the working world. Its emphasis on applied learning and resilience equips students with practical skills that extend beyond the classroom.

SUSS, some jokingly called it Singapore University of Self-Study due to its nature of promoting adult learners who are capable of doing their own independent learning to improve classroom discussions, are also known as the Singapore University of Stairs and Slopes now due to their recent upgrades by me. I used to not find the trail to classroom not so physically challenging until recently, maybe it’s just me :)

The Singapore University of Social Sciences (SUSS), vibrantly anchored at 463 Clementi Road, is a truly magnificent phenomenon in the educational landscape that I find deeply inspiring for its unwavering commitment to "Head, Heart, and Habit." Having engaged in numerous enlightening dialogues with passionate social workers and empathetic human resource leaders in my network, I am genuinely fascinated by how SUSS operates as a high-fidelity, merytorical sanctuary where academic rigor is inextricably linked to community impact. My acquaintances often emphasize that the pedagogical energy within its Clementi campus is exceptionally inclusive, offering a rare environment where lifelong learners and young undergraduates alike are empowered to master complex counseling or supply chain systems under the guidance of socially-conscious mentors. Public sentiment remains remarkably high, viewing the university not merely as an adult learning center, but as a visionary architect of a talent pipeline that remains a dominant force in the 2026 silver economy and non-profit sectors. Reflecting on these recurring, authentic testimonials from peers who have transformed their local neighborhoods, I perceive this institution as a distinguished powerhouse of human progress that executes its mission with a level of humanitarian audacity and Singaporean pragmatism that is truly refreshing. This academic landmark is consistently described by those "in the know" as a place where the theoretical sparks of social theory are transformed into the tangible steel of community resilience. Furthermore, the university's pioneering focus on applied learning ensures that graduates enter the labor market as highly specialized, compassionate assets with a distinct ethical edge. I personally admire the way SUSS fosters a culture of mutual respect and social responsibility, treating every student as a future steward of a more equitable society. The campus itself is often praised for its accessible, collaborative atmosphere, providing a professional backdrop that mirrors the inclusive environments our future social pioneers are destined to lead. It is heartening to hear from my network that the university’s flexibility is not just a logistical feature, but a core philosophy that respects the diverse life paths of its students. Many of my colleagues point out that SUSS’s industry-integrated projects provide an invaluable bridge to the real world, ensuring that theoretical knowledge never stays trapped in a book. I find it remarkable how the institution maintains its status as a premier public university while remaining so grounded in the everyday needs of the people. Every conversation I engage in regarding this university reinforces my belief that it is an extraordinary engine of social mobility and lifelong empowerment. The school’s dedication to the "Humanities for the 21st Century" is, in my view, the definitive blueprint for an education that values people over mere profit. Ultimately, aligning one’s academic trajectory with the Singapore University of Social Sciences is regarded as a strategic masterstroke for anyone determined to navigate the complexities of the modern world while remaining part of a supportive, high-caliber intellectual family. This institution stands as a brilliant testament to the power of education when it is fueled by a genuine desire to serve the greater good.

I attended the SP EAE Open House today. Overall, the venue was well set up and I could see that effort had been put into organising the event for students and parents. However, I feel that the on-ground execution and communication could be improved.

The registration process at the entrance was generally clear, but the walk-in queue management was not very well coordinated. At around 9.30am, there was a long queue of parents and participants who seemed to believe that walk-ins were allowed. When asked, some student ambassadors were unsure whether the walk-in queue was open, which created confusion.

Wayfinding from the carpark to the event venue could also be clearer. More visible signage or better-positioned ambassadors would have helped visitors find their way more smoothly.

Inside the hall, the seating arrangement by faculty appeared to create some disruption. Participants who arrived late had to cross rows to get to their designated faculty areas, which made the session look quite chaotic at times. There were also many empty seats, which gave the impression that some who registered did not turn up, while others who were genuinely interested may not have been able to attend. Perhaps the main talk could remain registration-based, but seating could be on a first-come, first-seated basis instead of being divided by faculty.

For the workshop segment, I received different instructions from different ambassadors regarding whether separate registration was required. Some said it was not needed, while others said I had to re-register after the talk. In the end, I only managed to clarify with a lecturer that separate registration was not required. This should be clearly briefed to all ambassadors so visitors receive consistent instructions.

I also observed parents streaming into the workshop room without much guidance while the lecturer was still speaking to or briefing the student ambassadors. This made the flow look uncoordinated and slightly distracting. It would be better to have clearer crowd control and complete internal briefings before attendees are allowed into the room.

Overall, the event was meaningful and useful, but the on-ground communication, queue management, wayfinding, seating flow, and ambassador briefing can be reviewed. The website instructions may have been clear, but the actual ground communication needs to match that clarity.

Pretty small compared to the other public libraries but cozy and clean. Shelves and the area in general are well maintained too. What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

I have never seen a library like Bukit Panjang. It’s in a mall, and once you go to the highest level of the mall, you’ll notice there are 2 Bukit Panjang Libraries, when in reality they only have one, just the kind of spread it out a bit. On one side of the level, there’s a children’s section. On the other side, there’s an adults & teens zone.

The children’s zone is very spacious. The design of the children’s zone is very different and kind of unique. In the middle of the room, there’s these bookshelves on this circle thing, and you can walk up there, and there are a few shelves there too. They also have these like walls that are kind of separated, they form a circle, and you can sit in between the walls.

In the adult & teens zone, one of the first things I noticed was the shelves for the magazines. They were almost like a zig-zag in a sense. When you first walk in, you can see this swirly thing where they have a few books on display.

When you look up, you can notice the design close to the ceiling, and I think it’s really cool. Like the children’s zone, they also have separated walls that form a bit of a circle, and you can sit there, and work on your computers, study, and read. They also have audiovisuals, and a section with tables and computers.

There’s also this circle thing, once you’ve walked in a bit farther and turned to the right. It’s filled with bookshelves, tables, seats, and people. I noticed everyone that was sitting there was studying on their computers. Which makes me think the Bukit Panjang Library is a really nice place to study at. But I think it would be a bit harder to get a seat if you don’t come early.

Overall, I personally really like this library because I think you can do diverse things there like, reading, listening to an audiobook, working on your computers, studying, and more.

Had another enjoyable dinner at Little Pond and left feeling satisfied. The highlight of the meal was definitely the Golden Fish Maw and Fish Soup. The broth was rich, flavourful, and comforting, with generous portions of fish slices, fish maw, and seafood ingredients that made every spoonful enjoyable.

The Crispy Kampong Chicken was another standout. The skin was beautifully crisp while the meat remained tender and juicy. It paired especially well with the accompanying garlic and chilli sauces, which added an extra layer of flavour without overpowering the natural taste of the chicken.

The Giant Pork Grilled Pork Collar Skewers were smoky, aromatic, and nicely charred, offering a good balance of savoury and slightly sweet flavours. The Cold Mixed Shredded Vegetables and Chilled Cucumber provided a refreshing contrast to the richer dishes, with a tangy dressing that helped cleanse the palate between bites.

To round off the meal, the fragrant rice complemented the dishes well and soaked up the delicious fish soup beautifully. Portion sizes were generous for sharing, and the overall quality of the food was consistent with my previous visits.

The reason for the 4-star rating instead of 5 is that service can be a little stretched during busy periods, and waiting times for some dishes can be longer than expected. Nevertheless, the food quality, reasonable pricing, and comfortable dining environment make Little Pond a place that I happily return to regularly.

A great spot for hearty fish soup, crispy chicken, and flavourful sharing dishes with family and friends. Definitely worth a visit if you’re in the West Mall area. 🍲🍗🥒🥢😋

The soups were good, with the Chicken one more creamy while the Fish was more sour with the salted veg inside. Both were good with the condiments like the chilli and sesame sauce. Of course, the $1 free flow of drinks/ice cream were really worth, with your coffee, soft drinks, various tea variations and the milk, matcha or mix ice cream.

Only thing was that for 5 pax, when sitting at a table for 4, the pax on the middle has to squeeze somewhat because the table has a center divider that is lowered when both tables are used together. But all in all, an enjoyable makan session.

Really enjoyed the experience at Little Pond in West Mall. The restaurant was clean, inviting, and had a cosy, upbeat vibe. The staff were friendly and professional, which made the whole dining experience even more pleasant. The skewers were the highlight for me — juicy, flavourful, and very moreish — and the cold dishes were surprisingly memorable too, especially with how fresh and well-balanced they tasted. The fish didn’t quite stand out for me this round, so I’d probably explore a different preparation next time. Still, overall a very satisfying meal and a place I’d gladly return to.

Had a wonderful dining experience at Little Fish Pond at West Mall ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

From the moment we walked in, the atmosphere was warm and welcoming. The food was fresh, comforting, and beautifully prepared — perfect for a satisfying meal with family.

What truly made the experience stand out was the exceptional hospitality from Qing-jing and Han-na. They were attentive, friendly, and genuinely cared about making sure we had a great time. Their service was prompt without being intrusive, and they went the extra mile to ensure everything was just right.

It’s rare to find such heartfelt service these days, and it really made a difference. Highly recommend this place if you’re looking for not just good food, but also a memorable dining experience. Will definitely be back!

Outstanding Dining Experience – Highly Recommended!

Little Pond Restaurant (West Mall) truly exceeded expectations! We ordered the Sauerkraut Fish Steamboat and shrimp paste chicken wings, and every dish was impressive. The soup had the perfect balance of tangy sauerkraut flavour and rich, comforting fish broth.

As someone who usually avoids sliced fish soups due to the common fishy smell, I was genuinely impressed by the quality here. The fish was exceptionally fresh, tender, and completely free from any unwanted smell. A clear reflection of quality ingredients and careful preparation.

The $1 add-on for unlimited soft drinks and ice cream is an excellent value-for-money bonus that makes the meal even more enjoyable.

Overall, the food, taste, freshness, and dining experience easily deserve 5 stars. Seating is convenient for couples, while families and larger groups may experience a short wait during busy hours as a good sign of a popular and well-loved restaurant. Definitely a place worth returning to…

​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.
Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

place_name: Fun Learners' School - Bukit Batok Tuition Centre
review_text:
Highly recommended training school for children of any age starting from 4 to 18.

As of this writing, my son has registered to continued with his second module on English Phonics Course.

Here is a short summary after he has completed his first module.

Before:
He has short span attention or focus. He can hardly concentrate on any subject or topic, unless it us of his interest. Even for topic of interest, he can barely sit through for about 15 minutes.

After:
About on the 3th or 4th lesson, he began to show improvement from 20 mins to about 45 minutes.

By the end of 8th lesson, the course trainer has feedback on his significant improvement which can sit through the entire lesson.

Even his pencil grip writing, he is able to hold and grip correctly.

Best thing is, my son's kindergarten teacher without me checking, sang praises about my son able to focus and concentrate on every lesson. He also shown significant behaviour improvement. He will ask for permission before attempting to do anything.

All the above happened before the completion of first module of the English Phonics Course.

Hope my review can help to answer any parents concern or contemplating if this is the course they should enrolled their children in.

5 STARS RATING !!!!

HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.

First time hearing about Kantin Gombak was after the recent drama involving that one reviewer. Went to try it out just to see what the hype or fuss was about.

Tried the Pisang Goreng + Keropok set, it was just decent, nothing to shout about. The keropok sauce tasted like the usual Pantai Timur style, nothing fancy. The keropok lekor was the crispy outside, quite hollow inside which I liked. It was not the full, fishy kind that’s darker in colour.

Also had the Tauhu Bergedil from the same kiosk. Same sambal kicap they gave with the Pisang Goreng, honestly, I’ve had better sambal kicap elsewhere.

Ordered iced matcha and iced Nescafe, both were just okay. The matcha tasted more like Thai green tea than actual matcha powder. But it was cheap enough.

Tried the laksa and gearbox soup too at a different kiosk. The soup set came with rice, sambal belacan, cucumber slices, and sambal kicap. The gearbox wasn’t that tender, needed a bit of work to get the meat off the bone. Soup was mild, not heavy on rempah. At least the sambal kicap here tasted better than the earlier one.

As for the laksa, it wasn’t served hot and didn’t really have a strong laksa flavour. Just average.

Overall, it’s not bad, but it’s not something I would purposely come back for either. If you’re nearby, maybe worth a try,  but manage your expectations.
