im assuming this outlet has a history of misplacing offer price to different items. i saw "2 for $3" mineral water and it was placed with ice mountain water so i took 2. when paying, the indian lady cashier told me the total price is $4.80. when asked, she said it was for 7/11 brand water??

put the prices properly?? youre alr causing a line to make payment and misplacing price signs too? $2.40 for mineral water not cheap btw.

The staff name Doris in this 7-11 branch located at bukit gombak mrt station is rude and no manners, i am looking for items at the chiller while she aggressively move items here and there and drop multiple items on the floor and place it back on the shelves of the chiller. She even drop the pre packed rice on the floor and hit my foot without even apologising. She just messing up the whole chiller items and just simply placing them randomly after picking it up from the floor. Terrible attitude and service.

“Unexpected Sparring Session”

Went to Bukit Gombak ActiveSG Gym to lift weights, not to witness the rise of the next Pay-Per-View champion. There’s this ONE kid wearing a Bukit Timah Rugby Highlanders tshirt with 400% confidence. Bro shows up in full boxing mode like he’s defending a world title sponsored by Milo. While the rest of us are quietly suffering on the machines, he’s in the corner throwing combos at the punching bag like it owes him money (lol). I’m on the deadlift trying to lift a new personal record with dignity and he’s beside me going “TSH TSH TSH” at lightning speed. At one point he glanced at me between rounds. That look said “Lightweight.”

Anyway, great gym. Equipment solid. Atmosphere? 10% fitness, 90% undercard fight night. Would return… but only after bulking enough to not get challenged by someone who still has a bedtime.

seriously too crowded. 6 treadmill machine and 1 is down for at least a month already. Regularly have to wait up to 30mins for your turn. 3-4 people rotating at any machine is common here. Although theres a guideline to not occupy the machine for more than 30mins , people regularly do so and the staff does nothing to regulate. Not sure why they need 2-3 staffs on duty here.

Conveniently located near MRT. Downside is that it is small, so limited equipment and is often quite crowded, even during non-peak times. Only one squat rack. It might not suit serious gym fanatics, but it's good for basic workouts so long as you don't mind waiting your turn to use the equipment.
The gym has recently replace with new machines and with more variety. More importantly the rate is reasonable and affordable. The staff are generally courteous and helpful.

This is only my second time here and I thought towel isn't mandatory. The Indian man at the counter half-shouted at me as if I had committed a crime. For a moment, I thought he was a bouncer. Some people are just unable to communicate with courtesy.
This is my second time and also my last. Anyway, this place is really too crowded. Can't expect much because it's freely sponsored by ActiveSG.

🤹Bt Batok Nature Park Playground is a sand-filled Children's Playground meant for children age 2 to 5 yrs.
🛝There are age appropriate Slides, Dome Climbers, Stepping Stones and Spinners.
🪣For toddlers, they can sandplay.
Kids will have an enjoyable time around the Playground.
🚻The Park's Public Toilet is just nearby, if need cleaning up.
🧙The Nature Park is wheelchair friendly, so all 3G may enjoy family bonding time together at the Playground.

Nice playground for the kids to play and run around. A stationary fitness area nearby to do exercises. Been there for many years, people are nice and friendly. Just don't hog the station too long so other users can make use of it. Please keep the area clean.
Wonderful place to spend time with Jesus. Quiet place for Prayer and meditation. Great place for exercises.
Establishing a park of this nature within the community zone constitutes an excellent strategy.
A good variety of equipment and space for kids (and bigger kids!) to use and enjoy.

Small and nice park. You just need to spend 10 min to 15 min for a walk and look.
If you stay in the centre area, not near to this place, I will not recommend it to you. But the view was nice.

Free entrance

Bukit Gumbak Hill is a hidden gem for nature lovers and hiking enthusiasts! There are several public toilet and rest points with benches, and the entire area is clean and well-maintained. I also appreciated the informative signboards about the local wildlife and plant species.

Whether you're looking for a peaceful escape from the city or a spot to enjoy nature's beauty, Bukit Gumbak Hill is a fantastic choice. Don't forget to bring plenty of water and wear comfortable shoes. Highly recommend a visit!
Nice lake but outside of Singapore. Beautiful landscape and also animals
Great place to explore. Very serene and natural. Hope the land is not taken for any future developments.

Nature preserve between Goodview Gardens Gombak Rise Hillview Mansion and Pavillion Park. Breezy greenery away from busy cityscapes including Pavillion Children's playground and Seng Chew Quarry. Walking distance to Bukit Batok MRT bus interchange Bukit Batok Town Park "Little Guilin" Bukit Gombak ActiveSG Stadium Hong Kah North Community Club Bukit Gombak Neighbourhood Centre Shopping Mall with plenty of dinning shopping banking Prime and Giant supermarkets PAP Community Foundation Hong Kah and amenities nearby. Friendly cosy comfy welcoming ambience for romantic intimates groups students expats locals visitors and families.

The community garden at Bukit Gombak Park showcases a mix of care and neglect. While many plots seem untended, a few stand out with thriving vegetables, spices, and lush greenery. A glimpse of dedication amid the wild!
a fresh air scenery and great view of many plants
Very nice place for a stroll
Nice place

I am writing this review after my daughter's two years' plus journey with Little Kinder Montessori, Hillview Center.
I still remember my daughter started her first school day on September 2023, right before she turning 2 years. It was our fortune to find this preschool and had a memorable time with all the teachers and children. I believe the principle Ms Yvonne is not only hosting a preschool, but also building a mini society which bonds all the patents, children and teachers. My child learnt a lot from this school and enjoyed her time there. I also got to know all the names in my daughter's class as we would like to talk about her daily life at home.
It was quite upset to ask my daughter to leave this school because we were moving house recently. Hope to meet all the friends again and all the best to you all!

Highly recommended childcare!
First impression was the owner, Yvonne, has a humble and meticulous approach with the enrolment and tried to understand my needs and concerns. From the moment I walked in, the centre gives a warm feelings and the teachers are very friendly.

Communication and feedback are very transparent and the team will try their best to manage my requests too. Teachers are caring and nurturing, they create a warm and welcoming environment that fosters learning and development.

My child loves going to this center every day and comes home happy, he also surprised me with some new skills or knowledges from time to time. I feel confident and at ease knowing that my child is safe and well-cared for in their capable hands.

Thank you Yvonne & Team for providing such exceptional care and support.
My best experience and mind at ease journey on my child's early education and well-being with Little Kinder. 👍

Two of my kids attend this school, one graduated two years ago and the other is in the nursery class. I love the school environment, the small class size, the daily outdoor play and occasional water play. The teachers are very dedicated and the school is very well managed by Yvonne. She will often keep us updated on the kids progress and sometime even send us photos on what they are up to. I really appreciate the personal touch. I was super impressed with the year end play they had for my son’s K2 graduation class two years ago. All props and costumes hand made by the teachers, super creative!! This school is like a second family for my kids, great environment for learning and growing.

Can’t agree more with previous reviewers. My daughter has attended this school since she was 18 months and now still going strong at 2.5 yrs of age. I love the healthy meals prepared at school, the open and timely communication with all the teachers and principal Ms Yvonne, the variation of activities they have for the children, and the outdoor space for playing and learning.

My girl comes back happy and likes attending school. She is also enjoying the extra weekly Kindermusik enrichment class (it’s optional) that we signed her up for. Her speech and vocabulary has developed by leaps and bounds since the very first week she joined the school—I remain very impressed by this.

Sometimes, parents are asked to be involved a little in the kids’ projects, and I find it a good way for us to remain connected and in tune with what the kids are doing in school. If only they had infant care at this branch!

My son went to little kinder Montessori for two years and I can't thank Yvonne and her team of lovely teachers enough. He loved going to school everyday and we as parents saw a sea change in his communication skills and confidence. It has a very cosy and homely environment and all the teachers are very soft spoken and loving.  I would definitely recommend this school for anyone living in this area.
Beautiful class room :)
nice and clean place to study
Where are the waffles
A comfortable learning classroom for 30 people.

The tutors are not only provided excellent teaching, but are also patient, friendly and dedicated to helping their students succeed. The learning environment is great as well. it's welcoming, supportive and conducive to learning. Highly recommend!

I was tutored by mr noel in 2022 for physics and absolutely enjoyed all the lessons there. I joined quite late and wasnt doing well for physics, but with the interactive and small group tuition sessions, my grades improved quickly and I did well for O levels! highly recommended for affordable and reliable tuition !!
super easy to get academic resources for all subjects + levels (from primary to jc) ; effective tutors + friendly admin who makes the whole experience better :)
Tutors are effective in communicating and teaching of concepts, using many resources to ensure sufficient practice and understanding.
I will definitely recommend Mr Jack Wang and his learning centre to other Parents as he have made my son from failing to good grade. 👍🏻
Nice food, reasonable price with quantity. HALAL !

Curry pok skin is not nice. 3 for $2.5. What a waste.
Only take away.
Terrible here. Seller is stingy as hell. I ordered two flavours for the waffle but look like the dry waffle. I have never seen someone so bloody stingy using a spoon to spread and
Hygience level in shop is very low. Cashier is wearing gloves to hand money over to customers,  not hygienic. Also, dustbins in shop are left open without lid.
Ew.... I saw someone touching their sole and didn't wash their hands and pass the bread to the customer... anyway their bread doesn't seems to be fresh!
Love their almond, walnut cookies. 1 for $1.60 or 3 for $4.50.

I had a really unpleasant experience at this 7-Eleven. The Indian staff member at the counter was extremely rude and dismissive. She kept me waiting for several minutes while she appeared to count cigarettes, and when I handed her the cash, she gave me a look that made me feel completely unwelcome. I expect at least basic courtesy when making a simple purchase, this kind of attitude is unacceptable for a customer-facing job.

The store is located near to school's. Very convenient a good place to get some chilled ice creams,sodas,snacks etc.
But the counter staff an old man is rude. Spoiled my day 02 Aug 2024 around 16:15hrs
Getting Ice creams for my son's and counter staff shouted at my son to change the ice cream at the counter saying that the so called ice creams that we
are getting is not on promotion and he shove the ice cream aside asking us to change it n this leads to confusion. What's with the promotions?!! with or without the promotions I still want to buy it. It's My Money My Wish
Counter staff need to learn how to be more polite and learn to talk properly. Not explaining by showing attitude shoving and shouting this does not work. Bad customer service.

Have been coming here for the past 4 years. Everything was good until recently an Indian female staff who was very SLOW and keeps taking her own sweet time at cashier. Also couldnt explain clearly how the Yuu points redemption work and I ended up not being able to redeem point after she said yes yes can redeem before I check out

Located near the neighborhood, it is a nice  place to relax after a long school day. It is quite a small outlet but it provides many things. It is also a good place to buy some snacks on the way home.  The small location makes it hard to navigate the store though
Everything here is good, but I wish they have the shake salad like some other 7-11 stores

A nice place for a coffee at the naturally ventilated sheltered space. The indoor hall is well used and host to many activities. It was an amazingly friendly sight. The central courtyard is an open area. Presently there are some building works at the front of the club. There are also well lighted tables for the residents to read newspaper, mostly occupied by the senior citizens who love the tactile feel of the papers. There are many seniors who likes to gather around to meet old friends.

The community center houses several function rooms, study corner, main indoor badminton court/hall (2x courts), and tuition centers. Vending machines and connected food court are situated outdoor. The outdoor colosseum has got badminton and basketball courts with a sheltered stage and public toilets (each male & female, very dirty).

A good place for some exercise and for students to study as there are free internet and lots of place to sit. Of course when HA phase over. There is also a coffeeshop behind, Food  Paradise, with various stalls to try out.

nonsense experience.. so much for singapore to become smart nation. they dont accept paynow when buying ticket for community event.. the receptionist asked me to go and take exact $15 cash to buy the ticket

I was asking if i can transfer to her and she could pay cash because only few tickets left and my parents wanted to attend the community event

I had to rush and take the money and rush back.. this experience sucks really hope we can move towards smart nation

Bukit batok should not lag behind because of cc not taking the initiative

This is so basic.. so disappointing attitude of the elderly staff. Hope MP can look into it

A very convenient location for a community club. There are eateries around and I loved the private study cubicles. Would have been better if there were direction signs for the elevator as the place is quite spacious and open.  You might miss the main entrance if you are coming from the Bukit Batok MRT side. But if you enter from the main entrance you should be fine. A very ell thought through building with lots of amenities.

It has been a long time since I last visited Guilin Park, and coming back was a pleasant reminder of how unique this place is in Singapore. The highlight of the park is the old granite quarry, which forms a dramatic rocky cliff overlooking the lake — it almost feels like a mini version of Guilin’s limestone landscapes

The walk to the quarry viewpoint is short and easy, making it a nice stop for a quick stroll rather than a long hike. The surrounding greenery, calm water, and rocky cliffs create a scenic and peaceful atmosphere, especially on a bright day with blue skies

Visited as the last stop of one of the CDC Trails. The last time I visited here was during a school geography field trip a few decades ago. A tranquil place to visit. Almost a mirror lake. Part of the Bukit Batok East Park Connector.
Worth the MRT trip out here. Yes, it's quickly seen...but it's a beautiful place!
Di sana nggak ada gunung Bromo ah, "jelek"

place_name: Starshine Montessori Hillview – Infant Care & Preschool
review_text:
My child attended N1 and N2 at Starshine Montessori and it was a wonderful experience.
The school environment is cozy and they have a nice outdoor playground as well.

The teachers are very supportive, caring and willing to put in extra effort to iron out any issues a child may be facing.
The curriculum is well rounded with extra enrichment like Brain Boosters lessons which were very helpful for the development my child. My child always looked forward to the water play sessions on Fridays as well!

Thank you Ms Ros and all teachers at Starshine Montessori for making our child's nursery journey meaningful and fun!

place_name: Starshine Montessori Hillview – Infant Care & Preschool
review_text:
Our daughter has been studying at Starshine from July 2024 to December 2025. As we have recently relocated to Boon Lay, which is closer to my workplace, we will be transferring her to a new school.
We would like to express our sincere appreciation to the entire Starshine team for their dedication and excellent support. During her time at the school, our daughter has made great progress in creativity, communication, and listening skills, and has learned to interact confidently with other children.
We are also thankful for the school’s well-rounded programs such as Brain Booster and Robotics Skills, which she truly enjoyed. The celebration days and other activities created a joyful and engaging learning environment.
We deeply appreciate the commitment, care, and professionalism of all the teachers and staff who have contributed to her growth. Thank you for providing such a positive and nurturing foundation for her development.

place_name: Starshine Montessori Hillview – Infant Care & Preschool
review_text: My children have been attending Starshine Montessori since January 2022. It first started with my daughter in 2022. My daughter entered the school in N2 and graduated in 2024. She thoroughly enjoyed her time at Starshine Montessori. Not only do they provide a fun and engaging curriculum, teaching reading, Maths and Chinese, they also have outdoor activities as they have their own outdoor playground, art and crafts, music and dance. The school also provides enrichment programs such as violin classes, Lego robotics and brain training, etc. During the time my daughter attended the school, her confidence and language skills improved significantly. She also learnt to be understanding and patient to younger children and to those with learning difficulties. While we did experience a change in ownership, we are thankful that this did not affect the daily operations of the school and everything was smooth and streamlined. The principal and core teachers are dedicated, as well as patient and kind. We liked the school and the small community so much that we decided to have our son enroll into the school last year. He is currently a student there and enjoys going to school everyday. He is very attached to the teachers, which clearly shows the excellence, patience and dedication of the teachers at Starshine Montessori. I would like to thank the school for their tireless efforts and care they provided my daughter during her time there, and the ongoing support and patience there show my son. Thank you Starshine Montessori.

place_name: Starshine Montessori Hillview – Infant Care & Preschool
review_text: My son loves the environment at Starshine Montessori and adores every teacher. Their patience has helped him overcome initial struggles with language and the new environment. Now, my child looks forward to going to school every single day. That’s the Starshine difference!

place_name: Starshine Montessori Hillview – Infant Care & Preschool
review_text:
We’ve had a wonderful experience with Starshine Montessori. Our 3-year-old attended for five months, and the growth we’ve seen has been amazing. The teachers are incredibly nurturing, patient, and genuinely passionate about what they do. They created a warm and welcoming environment where our child felt safe, confident, and excited to learn every day.

We especially appreciated how they fostered independence, creativity, and a love for learning through hands-on activities and thoughtful guidance. Even after just a few months, we noticed improvements in communication, social skills, and problem-solving abilities.

We’re so grateful to Miss Ross and her entire team for making our child’s first school experience such a positive and enriching one. Highly recommend to any parents looking for a caring and inspiring Montessori program!

Things we appreciate about the school:

1. clear and timely updates about curriculum and school work- my child has blossomed since joining the school in k1. she speaks enthusiastically about the activities in school and the updates also help us engage her at home.

2. holistic approach: the school prides itself in coding and my child also enjoys the AI chinese and math sessions. not only that, she also enjoys the hands on activities and learning journeys planned.

3. enclosed classrooms: dedicated classrooms for each class so that learning can take place effectively.

4. caring teachers and management: we sense that they care immensely about the holistic development of a child.

5. events like CNY, christmas etc celebrated with the involvement of parents - meaningful activities planned for us.

Thank you for providing a safe and nurturing space for our child!

My daughter absolutely loves the preschool. Huge thanks to Principal Ms. Pat, Admin Ms. Sharon, and Teachers Ms Jasmine and Zhang Laoshi for being so caring and supportive. She’s had amazing opportunities, including interschool and overseas robotics competitions in Osaka, a Chinese storytelling competition, and exciting termly field trips. Her confidence and curiosity have grown tremendously. She has built wonderful friendships, learned to work in teams, and developed a genuine love for problem-solving. Thank you!

We are truly happy and grateful to have chosen ChildFirst@Hillview for our son. Although he has only been attending for a month, we already feel a strong connection with the school.

From the very first visit, we were impressed by the bright classrooms, safe and well-maintained facilities, and—most importantly—the warm smiles from every staff member. During the first week, I noticed that every teacher and staff member could call my son by name, which made us feel truly welcomed and cared for.

This is my son's first time in kindergarten. At first, he cried so much that he didn't want to go to school. But the teachers fully embraced his mood, talking gently to him, patiently comforting him, and even staying by his side throughout the nap. We really appreciate your concern, it means a lot to us. It is clear to us that the teachers here are not only professional, but really kind and compassionate.

We feel very fortunate to be part of the ChildFirst@Hillview family. Thank you for all the love and dedication you give to the children every day.

We are so grateful for the wonderful experience our son has at ChildFirst, Hillview. The dedicated teachers genuinely care about each child’s growth, creating a nurturing and supportive environment that makes learning fun and engaging. The innovative curriculum not only builds a strong academic foundation but also fosters creativity and independence. Seeing our child thrive, grow in confidence, and develop a love for learning has been truly rewarding. Highly recommend this preschool to any parent looking for a caring and enriching early education experience!

My child has enjoyed Childfirst, having transferred over from a previous school. The teachers care very much for the children, and the curriculum is rigorous. The school regularly engages us parents, and we are deeply involved in our child’s education journey. The school puts in a lot of effort to scaffold their lessons and plan varied and engaging lessons. Thank you, Childfirst!

After 15 years, NJC's boarding school (only) was officially closed in 2024 to return the land for state use.

These images hope to honour the memories of those 500 students and teachers boarded there every year

For more at #shuutur

Very disappointing. The secondary level teachers dont really teach. I was expecting some principles to be taught in class so I kept quiet and observed my kid's progress. Wasn't taught. Disappointed. They also got 1 alumni to teach while waiting for her A results. Teachers not teaching in way that can be understood. The school relies on private tuition and hard self study. A few teachers talks disrespectful like are sarcastic. Student prefers the previous principal

One of the famous schools in Singapore and undoubtedly with lots of heritage, history and culture. As you step into the school you feel the nostalgia and history working together to build a wonderful, hopeful and resilient new generation one after the other.
Does the system teach students basic etiquette these days? Or is it all about scoring well in exams and about rankings and academic achievements?

Shoes and shoelaces that are garish in nature are not allowed. W school, W people, W teachers, and most importantly, W chillies, popcorn and math.

Everyday I go to school and I look forward to seeing my math work. The piles and piles of math homework does not do itself. It sure is great experience and practice!!!

The accidental cohesive nature of this school is truly great. All that gleams is not gold. The equal funding given to the aesthetics and science departments are truly interesting.

I recall the days I went to NJC. Oh my, it has been quite a bit... I can recall the days we got our exam results back. I remember the smiles our teacher tried to hide from the students when they gave us those earth-shatteringly wonderful results. They smiled so wide, so wide we could barely see the tears forming in their eyes as they looked at our shattered expressions in pride.

The teachers love teaching, sometimes a little too much. I see their beautiful teary-eyes smiles upon their weary faces whenever they step into class. I see it, I feel it, I know it, it is ingrained into each and every one of their fabric of reality. As an alumni of this school, I missed the days of boarding. The chaotic thwacking of doors whenever we closed a door was truly inspiring.

The myths are not true. We do not have a leaky roof. In fact, it is the best roofs of all. The roof's whole is about winning. In fact, it is so winnable that it has always allowed water through it. It has never lost its purpose in letting water through.

One of the key problems today is that math is undervalued in this society, and that there is too much emphasis on the pursuit of arts. What even is the use for arts? Useless pining? Useless uselessness? Useless emotions? None of these things are needed. Join NJC, for you will see the world for what it is, through good, or through better, or through best, and that, is through math.

Our integrated programme is good. No, the best at INTEGRATING!!!! Our streak of streaky teary students crying tears of joy at the sight of their beautiful eggs received on their math papers is unmatched. One must join this institution to truly understand the meaning of life. For it is to MATH.

I truly apologise for this accidental cohesive multicultural heritage-ridden serviced and honoured post. NJCians truly deserve more math homework.

Hillview MRT is a very well-connected station that provides easy access to nearby restaurants, malls, and various other facilities. A major highlight is the presence of sheltered walkways, which are a lifesaver if you forget your umbrella during unexpected weather elements. We used this station to head to The Rail Mall, and it served that purpose extremely well.

This MRT Station is my "home" MRT Station - I live nearby at Dairy Farm Estate, which is just minutes' walk away. I use this MRT Station to travel to virtually all over Singapore with some journeys lasting more than an hour. That said, more regularly, I use this MRT Station to travel to the Beauty World area or the Bukit Panjang area - for shopping and "makan." So as you can imagine, I don't know I would do, if this MRT Station was never built.

My favourite place, almost visit every day. The old train track remind the history, Hillview is the charming place and also is the only one area having the mountain view. Suitable for jogging and walking.
The interior is very cool and calm and it’s the third station so you can easily get to Bukit Panjang quick.

Hillview MRT Station is modern, clean, and usually quite peaceful. Being on the Downtown Line, it’s easy to travel straight into town, and the station layout is simple and clear. It’s conveniently located near HillV2 and several residential areas, making it great for quick access to food and daily essentials. Trains are frequent, and the station never feels overly crowded. Overall, a comfortable and reliable station for commuters in the Hillview area.

Near to the MRT, there’s a Salvation Army and Methodist church which has a music classes. (There’s raffles gymnastic class in this area too.)
If you want to shop or dine, you should try the rail mall (5mins walking distance) or Hillview 2 (about 12-15mins walking distance).

Bus this mostly seems a residential area so there’s not so much a special thing here actually but the old Malayan railway at the rail mall is worth to visit!

Downtown line is the best!
Comfortable seats in trains.
Smooth rides.
Convenient to get to various places.
Carpark available next to the station (chargeable). New station for the Downtown line. Clean and easy accessable
Easier access to the Rail Corridor, previously the only way to get here is by bus.
Quiet... Upper class SES neighbourhood.. Good eateries around rail 🚈 mall

Spacious and vibrant dining venue with a great atmosphere, especially in the evenings. Wide variety of food options available, making it a good spot for groups with different cravings. Tried the Thai stall and it was really good 😋🇹🇭 — flavourful, well-executed dishes with fresh ingredients. The sotong and prawns were especially fresh and nicely cooked. The live entertainment also adds to the overall experience and creates a lively, enjoyable vibe 🎶✨ Seating is comfortable and the place is generally well-maintained. A nice spot to gather with friends or family for food and drinks. Worth visiting if you’re in the area.

Had a pretty solid time at Timbre+ Hillview. The space feels spacious and modern, with a nice mix of indoor comfort and open communal vibes. Conveniently linked to the new CMPB and near Cashew MRT, so it is surprisingly easy to get to. There is also a nice bar within to enjoy karaoke and also drinks. If you prefer a quieter corner, there are also sufficient seats at the other side without the crowd of the live band.

Good variety of food options and the live music gives the place a lively atmosphere without being overly noisy. Nice spot to chill with friends over dinner and drinks, especially at night when the crowd and energy pick up. Still feels relatively new so some stalls are still opening progressively, but overall the place has a lot of potential to become a solid hangout spot in the west.  ￼

雞多多 @ Timbre+ CMPB

Made a special trip all the way from the east just to try this stall, highly recommended by a friend who works here — and it didn’t disappoint. Prices are very reasonable, especially considering it’s located in an atas coffeeshop where most stalls are on the pricier side.

The steamed chicken is tender and juicy, perfectly complemented by their fragrant onion-ginger sauce. The roasted chicken is equally good, with a nice balance of flavour and pairs especially well with their chilli sauce. Definitely worth coming back for.

Live band daily 7pm sharp. Air conditioning is ok, clean environment and toilets. Lots of local delights available. Good environment for families with elderly, working adults and children. Live band not overly loud.

No gst no service charge.

Came on a Sunday 7 plus, their last set of singing is about 8+. Most shop close at 9+.

Sunday’s singer vocal is really great. Not overly crowded
Nice ambience, clean. Some stalls not open yet. 酸菜鱼面 is good. Portion is big👍

We had lunch at IO Italian Osteria at HillV2 and were really impressed by the quality of the food. The ravioli was beautifully made with great depth of flavour, the braised beef cheek was tender, and the truffle schiacciata was aromatic and addictive. Every dish felt carefully executed and comforting.

What truly elevated the experience was the service. Mega and Jit were exceptional — warm, attentive, and genuinely hospitable. They checked in at just the right moments and made sure we were well looked after throughout the meal, and topped up our water regularly.

All in all, a wonderful lunch with excellent food and standout service. A restaurant well worth a visit.

Traditional stuffed roman schiacciata (truffle one) is fantastic, crispy and flavourful. Amatriciana (bacon & tomato, this is non-spicy) pasta is simple but done really well.
Dolce salata (mixed salad) is yumny and refreshing
Skip the prawn & asparagus taglioline though, nothing special imo.
Also skip the buttered & fried cod (not pictured) as the batter was way too thick.

Place is huge and suitable for groups. Feels like an authentic Italian place. Lovely natural lighting in the daytime.

Visited June 2026

Ordered the following to share for 2 pax.

1. Stuffed Roman Schiacciata with truffle
2. Burrata with Parma ham and tomatoes
3. Lasagne
4. Maritati pork sausage and saffron
5. Pistachio Tiramisu
6. Black Forest cake

Food served were wholesome and tasty. Place has large floor to ceiling windows letting in alot of natural light during the day. Place is lively and tables might be abit too close to eachother depending on where you’re seated. Service is warm and efficient. Definitely a gem for people staying around the area.

Bill cake up to around $157 with a $5 bottle of still water.

We have been loyal customers of this restaurant for quite some time, and it never disappoints. The food is consistently delicious and always keeps us coming back.

We would also like to give a special shout-out to Rajes, Mega, and Siva for their excellent service. They are always warm, attentive, and make us feel welcomed every visit. Their friendly service is one of the reasons we enjoy dining here so much :)

Highly recommended!

The ambience truly made us feel like we were in Italy cozy, relaxing, and perfect for a memorable dining experience.
The staff were very friendly and accommodating, and the food was absolutely delicious. Everything we ordered was so good! Highly recommended for anyone looking for great food, wonderful service, and authentic Italian vibes. Definitely worth coming back to.

Makan Grill (Charcoal Western & Local Grill)

Such a great find at Hillview Terrace! The charcoal grill makes all the difference—every dish has that lovely smoky, authentic taste.

We tried the crispy fish fillet, glazed salmon, half chicken, and satay—all juicy, well-seasoned, and satisfying. The prawn aglio olio was flavorful too, and sides like fries, onion rings, and coleslaw were fresh and generous.

Bonus: they serve cold beer that pairs perfectly with the grilled food! Great value ($10–20), friendly service, and a relaxed vibe. Ideal for a casual meal or chill session. Will definitely return again!

Masarup!!!

The food was absolutely delicious, and the service was very friendly. We ordered the steak, lamb, dry laksa pasta, grilled corn, and chicken wings.

The steak and lamb were incredibly tender and paired perfectly with the yakiniku sauce. I really loved the dry laksa pasta—it was great that we could adjust the spice level to our preference. The grilled corn was served with cheese and chili flakes, which made it so flavorful and satisfying.

As for the chicken wings, I initially thought they would be small since they were skewered, but they were actually full-sized wings. The charcoal grilling gave them a wonderful smoky flavor.

Our favourite is laksa pasta💜
I’ll definitely visit again. Thank you for the great experience! 😍

gotta try it to really experience the charcoal grilled food! its superb💯✨ all skewers taste amazing (truly charcoal grilled) & super affordable ($1.20-$1.50 per stick).

we also had the pork chop, garlic pasta, fries, tater tots, breaded chicken... & intending to try even more from the menu🍽️ overall food is affordable, yummy & portion is decent👌🏻

A newly opened hidden gem!
Charcoal grill finds in as low as $1.

Food is flavourful, rich and super worth the price.
From Main dishes to pasta, side dishes down to sauces.. it is purely homemade, her own recipe

Owner/chef is a filipino ex jr sous chef of famous restaurant in Ion's sky scraper FLNT, was an ex head chef of DC cafe at Ngee Ann City and before she opened her own stall, she was with AP cafe at Raffles Hotel as part of the pastry team.

Flowery words aren't enough to explain her cooking. I highly recommend you to go there and try out her love, her passion.

Had the prawn laksa noodle and it was really nice and flavourful. The prawns were fresh, and the noodles were well coated with the laksa sauce. Overall, a good meal.

The stall owner was also very friendly. Would definitely come back again to try other items

This neighbourhood shopping mall is just ten minutes' walk from where I live along Dairy Farm Road. I love coming here for the choice of shops therein - in particular, the CS Fresh (where I do my grocery shopping), the 7-Eleven (where the offers here seemed wider and different from other 7-Elevens) and the many eateries serving a very wide variety of cuisine, from Chinese, to Western, to South Asian. So even if it's not a big shopping mall, it more than meets my dining and shopping needs.
It's a residential place with some eating places, services and a cold storage. Nothing special.

A very decent destination for F&B, amenities, grocery or to bring your dogs out to walkabout. I will not really call it a mall per se, but more of a community area located within the lush greenery of Hillview. It’s about 7mins walk from Hillview MRT so it’s pretty convenient but when it rains i will expect footfall to be even lesser. Carpark is really poorly designed in my opinion - spaces are not maximized and the lots are just simply not sufficient. Turning radiuses are also very tight at certain corners. That said, I do enjoy the breeze and serenity around the area. There’s also a couple of really good Italian and Korean options within this establishment.  A 24/7 Anytime Fitness is also located here to cater to the residents in the area.

Find it lacking in the crowd pulling effect like other malls. Maybe due to the size.  The management is doing something right by having live performance which my wife thought was good. The Christmas decoration was good too.
Keep up the good work but more could be done to pull in the crowd.

The only mall serving the upscale premium Hillview residential area. There is a different feel here if you are comfortable with the heartland malls. Holland shopping area may come close but not close enough. Interesting place.

Since moving back to the west from the east, Sheng Siong has become one of my usual supermarkets that I frequently get my groceries from. The supermarket is spacious with a wide variety of selections, and everything is always neatly arranged. The vegetables and fruits are fresh as well.

The staff there are also very friendly, especially two ladies whom I encountered.  Darchana and Santos are very helpful and professional. Darchana always smiley and very helpful whenever assistance is needed. Small gestures like these really make the shopping experience more comfortable and pleasant.

I was in a rush so I didn’t manage to take more pictures, but I’ll update with more photos the next round.

Cashiers are fast and friendly. Will remind customers if they are Chas or Pioneer on the designated days.

The 2 storey Sheng Siong is 24 hours. Appreciate the long operating hours.

Peaceful shopping can be done after mid night.

Early in the morning, there's staff to help butcher pork to ur desire. Choose a piece of meat and they minced for u. Pork Belly if not much people, they can slice it for u.

Fish counters operate till later timing then pork. Large selection of prawns, fish, crabs.

Outdoor area, there's plant section, veg section and promotion section.

Variety is huge!

24-hour supermarket at West with a wide range of food & many varieties. Never worry about being unable to buy fresh seafood cod this outlet 24 hours yet can find almost everything we need. From food to household items, too

I love supermarkets, especially the bigger ones. I miss the old Sheng Siong Supermarket at Tekka Mall, which had unfortunately closed years ago.
This outlet seems rather big to me; Spacious with a great selection of affordable daily essentials. A really good place for grocery shopping.

** 4% special discount for seniors (Singaporeans/ PRs) 60 and above, every Tuesday and Wednesday.
**The Tem Tem Protein Tempeh Chips sold here are suitable for vegetarians, healthy and super delicious. Get yours now for the coming Chinese New Year. You’re worth pampering.

It is actually a nice supermarket for shopping groceries. Best to avoid mornings. If you go on the afternoon, it is quite pleasant. It is large: with two floors of retail space. The prices are reasonable and the staff are really polite and friendly especially those at the checkouts. The things I dislike:
-using the trolleys. Sometimes you feel that they do not even sanitise them as you see bits of rubbish left over from the previous users.
-fish area as the fishy smell is quite strong
-sometimes at the entrance it is congested with unloaded goods which block access to the carpark.

Such a nice Cold Storage in Hillview.

The roast meat display is always well stocked with delicious well-prepared meat, kept hot, with the rotisserie machine doing its work in the background.

The iced seafood counter has a plethora of jumbo prawns, lobster tail sometimes, and a abundance of fresh fish.

The selection of spices and sauces is second to none; one can find some of the rarer vinegars and marinades here.

Champagne and wine selection is extensive, of quality, and sometimes they give hefty discounts.

Drinks and soft drink selection is likewise varied and full of options.

Snacks galore at this outlet; they sell anything from niche designer snacks to more recognizable homegrown brands.

Temperature kept very cool and store is very clean.

This CS Fresh (or Cold Storage) supermarket in HillV2 mall is popular with residents living nearby at Hillview Rise and possibly also with residents in the Hillview area and across the road so to speak in the Dairy Farm area where I am from. It is quite a big supermarket with a very wide variety of products and it is very well stocked. Perhaps the only downside to this supermarket is that it is on the second level of HillV2 mall, making large purchases therefrom quite a challenge for those without personal transportation. Anyway, it is a much welcome supermarket in the area as there is no other supermarket nearby.

UPDATE OF 12 JUNE 2026
I bought today, the roasted whole chicken and some Australian small tomatoes, which were on special offer. I want to commend the counter lady who took my order of the roasted whole chicken - her name is Jessie. She was most polite and helpful.

Slightly higher end CS but on a smaller size scale. Think the CS at rail mall has more variety at better prices but if you’re staying in the area, this would be your best bet. I like the “reduced the clear” area - which has many good steals. Service is pretty average - and you’re much better off using the self check out counter.
Big and great shopping place. Different from other cold storages. Fresh meat and seafood items. The bakery food was also fresh and good 👍.

It’s disappointing that CS Fresh, which advertises fresh products, falls short of that promise. Compared with NTUC Finest and Seng Siong, their fruits and meats are noticeably less fresh. We recently purchased salmon that was stale, and fruits like pomegranates and peaches are frequently sold overripe. I’ve also found expired items on the shelves. Worse, they are more expensive! Ironically, the bad reviews are floodingin the recent months. The management better do something about it before there’s food poisoning. If not for the friendly staff, I’ll give this review a 1 star.

Came early this morning at about 5.50 am thinking to just grab a sandwich to make self payment, and the indian staff just brushed me off saying oh sorry im mopping you have to wait for me for a bit. When clearly she was done and was just going for a quick break. Like how long would it take just for her to open the store for 1 more minute to let me get breakfast? Because of her, i had no breakfast this morning. Thank you to whoever you are!

I have had the worst experience ever at this 7/11 thanks to the Store Manager, Julie. There were two instances:

1. I ordered an iced americano, and when the drink was done, the cup cracked (maybe due to the rapid heat difference), which indicated a defect in the product. I asked Julie for help and she just sarcastically laughed at me saying “too bad, you’re just too unlucky, just drink it fast”. She didn’t even order any tissue or plastic to help me with the leaking drink

2. I placed an order via EasyCollect, and I came before she finished packing the items. I just told her “I’m here for the EasyCollect”, and then she just scolded me “it’s not done yet. Why are you here so early?” and kept on complaining to me for 10 minutes. Afterwards, she told me that she had finished packing, even though she forgot to pack an item. I asked her “is this my order? Are you sure?” And then she got angry at me, even though clearly she forgot an item. After I pointed it out, she did not even apologize. And she kept on urging me to show the collection QR code, which I had not received due to her forgetting to scan and pack that one item

She was extremely rude, condescending, and impatient. And when I reciprocated her ways, she got very offended and angry. How is she even Store Manager when she has 0 customer servicing skills and 0 proficiency in the EasyCollect system? Please fire her

Can attest to the experiences of the previous reviewers, the overnight store clerk seems to be perpetually "out for a break". Once or twice maybe Im just suay but this is my third time catching the staff MIA. Was just asked by a malay guy, who turned out to be the staff, to wait for him 10mins while he is chilling away at a bench outside the store. 🤦 Idk who the owner is but you really need to have a serious talk with your employee.

This auntie lady here is always angry at the customer.  She thinks customers are always perfect, especially putting money to dash .  She is always in a hurry  then if you recount your money to give her she will be angry and become mean to the customers.

terrible experience. i was left waiting there for over an hour because the sign hung by the door stated "15 minutes break". end up the staff had no show and literally evaporated like everyone else in my life.

there was a whole group of us standing outside like we were about to revolt against this unprofessional display of service by the 7-11 staff.

end up we all began losing confidence in this staff's appearance and decided to leave 1 by 1. i mean seriously, i just wanted to get an energy drink - didn't expect to be draining my energy both physically and mentally just by standing there instead.

all in all, terrible.

On 28 April 2026, my family and I visited your swimming facilities with the simple intention of letting my niece enjoy some time at the pool. Unfortunately, the entire experience was ruined by the unacceptable behaviour of one of your lifeguards, Mr Tang.

While we fully understand that safety rules must be enforced, there is absolutely no excuse for the rude, hostile, and unprofessional manner in which Mr Tang spoke to us. From the very beginning, his tone was confrontational and disrespectful, which is completely unacceptable for someone in a customer-facing role.

The issue regarding my wife wearing a cotton shirt was blown completely out of proportion. There was no attempt to explain the rule calmly or politely—only an aggressive and unnecessary escalation. We have visited many other swimming facilities and have never encountered such behavior over a minor issue.

What is even more concerning is that we witnessed Mr Tang treating another family in the same rude and aggressive manner. This clearly shows a pattern of behavior, not a one-off incident.

This kind of conduct reflects extremely poorly on your management and standards. Staff representing your facility should be capable of enforcing rules without being disrespectful or confrontational.

I strongly urge management to take this matter seriously and address Mr Tang’s behavior immediately. No customer should have their experience ruined due to such unprofessional conduct.

We came here to bring my niece for a swim, it was our first time visiting this pool and we were really looking forward to a nice family outing.

Unfortunately, the experience was completely ruined by one of the lifeguards.

I was standing in the baby pool, which was only up to my mid-calf, keeping an eye on my 2years old niece while my brother was going down the slide. I wasn’t even swimming, just supervising. I was wearing board shorts, a bikini top, and a cotton T-shirt as a cover-up. I got called out by the one of the lifeguard rudely for not wearing “proper attire.” His tone and approach were completely unnecessary and honestly quite disrespectful.
Not wanting to escalate things, I simply got out of the pool. But shortly after, I witnessed him being just as rude to another family. A Chinese family had just arrived, and their slippers were slightly over the poolside line, instead of politely informing them, he used his feet to shove their footwear aside and spoke to them in a very harsh manner. He even questioned whether they were members of the clubhouse, which felt inappropriate and uncalled for.

We later spoke to the security on duty and were told that this lifeguard, Mr Tang, has already received multiple complaints about his behavior. That honestly says a lot.

It’s such a shame because the facility itself seemed nice, but staff attitude makes all the difference. Basic courtesy and professionalism should not be too much to ask for. This experience left a really bad impression, and I would think twice before coming back.

The pool itself is nice, but the staff member at the entrance was quite rude. I politely asked for re-entry after stepping out briefly to buy a drink from the vending machine, but was met with unnecessary hostility.

Anyway, there’s no way management will address any concerns—they simply don’t take customer feedback.
Rude lifeguard, doesn't allow my kids to swim in the competition pool after their swimming lesson

Public swimming pool but Sunday can’t enter. 🤦🏻‍♂️ those membership holders only allowed in Sunday
Mentioned clearly lah.. from far come here after kids disappointed 🤦🏻‍♂️🤦🏻‍♂️🤦🏻‍♂️
Our 1210 show was delayed until 1245 & also required a hall change. Luckily we were compensated with a complimentary pass at the end.

Shaw Theatres, Lot One is a convenient neighbourhood cinema located right in Choa Chu Kang, perfect for residents who don’t want to travel far for a movie. The cinema has multiple halls of moderate size, offering a cosy movie-watching experience. While it doesn’t have IMAX, the sound system and screen quality are still very good for regular screenings.

The seats are comfortable, with enough legroom, and the halls are kept clean.
The staff are polite and efficient, making the ticketing and snack counter process smooth. Being located inside Lot One mall, it’s easy to grab a meal or do some shopping before or after the movie, which adds to the convenience.

Shore Theatre (Choa Chu Kang Lot 1 Shopping Centre): A Convenient and Comfortable Movie Experience (4/5 Stars)

Rating Breakdown:

• Service: 5/5 (Friendly and helpful staff, went above and beyond)

• Ambiance: 4/5 (Clean and comfortable, good air conditioning)

Overall: 4/5

Donkey and I were in the mood for a movie treat, and we decided to cycle from Punggol to Jurong Jem for the movie.  Unfortunately, we found out that the cinema was closed down. We decided to head to Choa Chu Kang Lot 1 Shopping Centre to catch a movie at their Shore Theatre instead.

When we reached the theatre, it was a small counter, but the lady server was very kind and helped us pick a movie time that was closest to what we wanted. We got there around 6 PM, and there was a movie starting at 6:40 PM.

After the movie, a really nice worker from the theatre guided us to the lift because the escalators were only going up, and we needed to get our trifold bike down to level 1. He even pointed out the exit that was only for theatre patrons. We good experience in this theatre.The air conditioning within the theatre was just right too.
Sit at last row which had big leg space. Theatre is clean and seat is comfortable.watched Grogu nice show and we paid only S$4.50 off peak hour for seniors ticket.
Quite cozy just that the halls are abit smaller compared to other Shaw theatres. I recommend picking seats nearer to the back for the best viewing experience as screens are elevated quite high.

Although I live "across the (Upper Bukit Timah) road" from this community club - meaning that my associated community club should be at Cashew Senja, this community club is my go-to for community activities and dining choices (there is a Malay cafe, a Korean restaurant, and a Domino's Pizza here) - for its proximity in the case of the former, and good food in the case of the latter. I also love the community club team here as they are very very friendly and very very helpful - in particular, a Ms Jaclyn Sim.

Nice community club in this premium location. Convenient to grab a bite or drink or have a proper sit down meal. It has the usual facilities in a community centre and still quite busy at 8pm night time though it is very quiet at the eateries.

Saw the interesting "castle" looking structure from a restaurant opposite and came over to check it out. Ended up discovering this heritage alley where we saw old telephone booth and relive and revived some retro feelings of life long past ...
v.clean ++++ CC common areas & toilets. reception was friendly and efficient with enquiries and purchase of tickets etc.

“Elizabeth Drive along Hillview Avenue is where what used to be Princess Elizabeth Estate, an old public housing estate built in 1951 to commemorate the wedding of Princes Elizabeth (later Queen Elizabeth II) to Prince Philip (Duke of Edinburgh).
The current Hillview Community Club is built on the grounds of this estate, where Princess Elizabeth Estate Community Center (CC) used to be…
This special connection to Queen Elizabeth lives on today at Hillview CC where a little gallery (Hillview Heritage Alley on the 4th floor of the Hillview CC) showcases the history of the estate and its vicinity.” - Ms Low Yen Ling, Member of Parliament, on Instagram
Tranquil small scale park for the private residential in this area. Suitable for wheelchair to go up as well which is a nice touch.
stupid

Overall, we had a pleasant experience at the restaurant. The restaurant was in a ballroom, with a cozy atmosphere. It was a Monday lunch and we were the only family there and the restaurant was empty. Honestly, we felt it was a little awkward but the food made our time worthwhile. The ala-carte buffet they offer has a wide assortment of vegetables, meats and delicacies, some of which can be ordered once only. There were crabs, cod fish, prawns, desserts and many more were served quickly when ordered. The birthday person (which was me) could eat free.

However, service could have been improved. When asking the waiter regarding the birthday discount, she could have been friendlier and gave a smile or perhaps wished me well. The serving of food was a little unprofessional and the waitress could have been more proactive in clearing plates, pouring drinks since there was nobody else and they were free. More effort could have been put in to make the extra mile to make our experience an even memorable one.

Other than that, the food is certainly commendable. The price is attractive and the restaurant is recommended.

I was there yesterday for the dinner buffet to celebrate one of my friend's birthday. 3 of us plus FOC for the birthday person. The taste of the food are okay but some dishes are cold and some are too dry after deep frying. The portion are small but just right so we can order a variety to try. They served the dishes quite promptly after we placed our order but to get their attention was a tad bit difficult as the tables are fully seated with customers but they're were only like 5 waiter and waitresses, except for the bubble soya which I have to keep asking for it when other tables can get theirs right after they were seated.

2025
Food is nice, better than many restaurants.
Worse dish : chicken, pretty tough and over cooked.
Best dish; salted egg prawn. The scallop and broccoli is pretty worth it. Huge portion and alittle spicy.

They still have ktv. The elderly loves it.

2026 April
Came back here for my grandmother’s birthday.

Food was nice in general. Had a room for 30 pax.

Went there for a weekend buffet lunch for $34.80 per-pax excluding gst and service charge fee of 10%.

Ordered almost the entire menu lol! Some hits and some misses but mostly good. Just to name a few of the good ones, e.g. fried rice, mee suah, smoked duck, garlic pork belly, char siew, teriyaki fried fish, chilli crab, cordyceps prawn, salted egg prawn, sweet sour pork, etc.

The cod fish, roasted chicken, sambal kangkung, buddha jump over the wall, thai fried chicken, wasabi roasted pork, braised pork in steamed bun, and fried spring roll, not so great.

The setting and dining environment is in a wedding restaurant. The props and deco are still there. Just something to take note of. Not a romantic setting that you would bring your gf unless you plan to get married after the meal.

They also offer free dining for birthday month, just show them your IC and that will do.

Overall happy with the experience, service is quite fast despite full house as they do not have shortage of waiter and waitress.

Book in advance to avoid disappointment.

Nice tasty food with small portion for buffet. So I can taste a variety of food. Seafood are fresh and well cooked. Enjoyed my meal, especially the chilli crab.
Lunch time very empty. Very friendly staff.

Not my style of condominium that I like to stay longer term. That said, it ultimately goes back to personal style. I personally find this condo a little tightly built, with too many small corners, too many things squeezed in a small plot of land, and doesn't give off the spacious vibes.

Security guards, the very first people that you meet, felt as if someone owed them alot of money. Not friendly, and giving off going through motion vibes.

Carpark was a little ridiculous, with visitors having to go to the 5th storey on a very tight-laned and confusingly designed carpark, before walking a long way to the residential block.

Club house was also hugely messy, with your event space being mixed with the communal space, having people walking in and out of your event.

Couple all of the above, with a torrential rain, and you will really feel like it's the actual Journey to The West.

Very disappointed with the management in this condo. Even when there are many empty lots at ground level on weekends, residents are still forced to park at Level 3 after 5pm and on Saturdays and Sundays. This is extremely inconvenient, especially on rainy days, and completely unnecessary.

To make things worse, there is a neighbour who constantly complains and asks security to wheel-clamp other residents’ cars, creating a stressful and unfriendly living environment.

It feels like there is no peace living here.

Management should seriously review these rigid rules and how complaints are handled. A home should be comfortable, not stressful and not feel like a PRISON
Beautiful condo. Feels like a resort. Very well managed and maintained.

So there are 4 buildings in the condo, and the visitor can only park to the mscp 5th floor(extreme left of the condo), that are at least few hundred metres apart from the extreme right unit that I'm visiting. There are plenty of surface parking but not opening for visitors. Management please do some work.
Doesn’t feel like a housing estate but prison. Amount of posters of do’s and don’ts plastered around the estate makes it feel like a prison.

During my stay at Guilin View, I found that several facilities were poorly maintained, and communication with management was slow. The overall upkeep did not match the expectations for a development at this price point. Prospective buyers should definitely inspect the property thoroughly and speak to current residents before making a decision.

Jeff is the best host in Guilin View. From the warm welcome to the thoughtful hospitality, Jeff made every visit enjoyable and comfortable. Friendly, attentive, and always ready to help—highly recommended if you ever get the chance to visit!

Gullin View is a cozy condominium nestled in the heart of River Valley.
Its strategic location offers easy access to amenities and transportation.
The propertyhas a range of facilities, including a swimming pool and gym.
Residents can enjoy stunning views of the surrounding landscape.
The apartments are spacious and well-designed, perfect for families.
Gullin View is surrounded by reputable schools and shopping malls.
The community is friendly and welcoming, making it ideal for expats.
It's a solid choice for those seeking a convenient and comfortable living experience.

Living at Guilin View has been an incredibly peaceful and fulfilling experience. There’s a sense of tranquility here that’s rare in singapore
— waking up to the calm view of Little Guilin, hearing birds instead of traffic, and watching the sun set over the water from the pool is something I’ve come to deeply appreciate.

The apartment itself is spacious and bright, with plenty of room to breathe. Everything in the estate is so well maintained — from the gardens to the facilities — it really feels like the management cares. The facilities are modern and thoughtfully maintained, making it easy to unwind or get a workout in without ever leaving the estate.

Despite the quiet, it’s still wonderfully connected. Bukit Gombak MRT is just a short walk away, and I love the convenience of the eateries and shops at Gombak Central.

This place isn’t just where I stay — it’s where I’ve felt at peace. Guilin View has become more than a home; it’s a retreat from the pace of daily life.

Very nice look at high levels to the cliff, the best I had seen in Singapore; pools well maintained and designs very good. Prices reasonable, near a couple of very nice places. Strongly recommend for one to pay a visit or live there.
A lovely stretch of park connector with shady trees and beautiful flowers in bloom.

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (HKN CC)
review_text: My kid has been at Hao Chinese Tuition Centre for a while now, and honestly it’s been great. The teachers really care and you can tell they love teaching. Sunny 老师 especially is so kind and patient, and my child adores her! After every lesson the teacher takes the time to tell us which words were tricky and what we should work on at home, which I really appreciate. My child looks forward to every class and can now talk to us in Mandarin with no problem at all. Thank you for making learning so fun! ☺️

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (HKN CC)
review_text: My daughter had started with Hao Chinese Tuition Centre since she was 4 years old. The teachers are all very dedicated and my daughter totally love attending the lesson. She is now able to communicate with us in Chinese. After the lesson, the teacher will go through with us what word is difficult and what to take note on the learning for the children. Jing 老师 is very gentle and my daughter love her! Thank you for making the learning enjoyable! ☺️

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (HKN CC)
review_text:
Even though I speak Mandarin to my daughter at home, she didn't do well for Chinese subject in school.
As it is her  PSLE the next year, I enrolled her to her 1st Chinese tuition after reading good reviews online about HAO.
Teacher Sunny is a very dedicated and polite Teacher who uses proper Mandarin. She will inform me via text or face to face on how my daughter was doing during tuition and engage in holistic approach to her studies.
My daughter has been attending HAO for almost a year plus minus.
I can see that there was improvement for her year end P5 exams last year. Hoping she will do better for PSLE.
Definitely recommending HAO !

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (HKN CC)
review_text: We’ve had a wonderful experience with this Chinese enrichment centre! My child has picked up many new Chinese vocabulary words and is now able to write some characters independently — something I didn’t expect to see so soon. The teachers are patient, nurturing, and especially skilled at helping children who may be anxious or teary during class. Their calm and encouraging approach made a big difference in helping my child settle in and enjoy learning. Highly recommend this centre to parents looking to build a strong Chinese foundation for their kids!

place_name: HAO Chinese Tuition Centre 好学堂 (HKN CC)
review_text: We are very happy to see our child's steady improvement since he joined from primary 5 and now he is in primary 6.  We appreciate the guidance and support from the teacher Sunny along the way, he has improved a lot especially the composition writing and we are encouraged by the progress made so far. We look forward to seeing our child continue to develop and do even better. 😊

Pig Trotter Vinegar will be perfect with a hard boiled egg - Dad

The Hakka Fried Rice was not dry and tasty with evidence of wok hei. The Cai Poh Hor Fun was very nice too. Hakka Nook is generous with their ingredients.

We thought we lost a good braised duck rice before but the new stall brought some high standard Hakka cuisine and we will continue to try other dishes in time to come.

The Hor Fun w Cai Poh, filled with ''wok hey' n the Hakka Fried Pork Belly w Braised rice w long beans, the pork's crispy n  flavourful.  The portion is more than enough for 2 to share... Will definitely return for the other dishes ..

Back to Bukit Batok playground and went back to our regular coffeeshop. There is a new Hakka store and I have ordered the Hakka Lei Cha. Even it is takeaway, it is still fresh and delicious after a few hours. The fragrance of the herbal green paste is superb, the taste is well-balanced and not bland at all. All the ingredients is perfectly cooked, the tau kwa is soft and flavourful, not those hard and cut gum and throat type. Overall, very comforting and easy to the stomach. I highly recommend the lei cha fans to gave it a try.

I ordered 3 dishes on 23 June 2026 at 7:00 PM. Only 2 of the dishes were delivered at 8:30 PM. We weren’t informed of the shortage before the food arrived. The stall sees it appropriate to change their customer’s order as they please⁉️
The food was very delicious  and lady  staff was very kind

I bought eggs today and u asked for a plastic bag however the worker who was a female was not paying attention to me and did not give me a plastic bag after she checked out my eggs I asked for a plastic bag and she gave me a plastic bag instead of putting my eggs in the plastic bag for me. I expect more attentiveness and consideration from your workers from now on. When customers are at the cashier checkout I expect the cashier to be responsible and pack the eggs into the plastic bag for the customers.

24 april 130am.

I need some change for the nearby laundry and I only have 50s and 100s. I decided to buy a small bottle of water instead of asking for small changes. The lady kept telling me "no small change" which i know its partially my fault for not checking before I left home. I explained to her I need small change for the machine as they don't accept 50s.

She stopped explaining and her face frowned and kept shaking her head. I mean if you're really that unhappy can you at least don't do it in front of customers? At least I buy something right?

GIANT your staff lacks training and professionalism

A perfect place to buy things near Guilin View and other residential condos and HDBs. The range is extensive, and you have a lot of space to roam around. The aisle space is also not crammed like in other supermarkets. Open 24/7, and probably the best time to purchase is after midnight when no one is around to disturb you. After Giant partnered with Meadows, the number of cheap and good quality stuff you can buy at any Giant supermarket storehouse increased considerably.
I went to purchase on Tuesday, and check with the staff whether have promotion for senior, the staff told me is Wednesday, after that I checked with website is Tuesday 😓so disappointed.

When to this giant supermarket and bought some stuff. Never ever think of such bad service do they have with one particular untie at the cashier.. she's the Chinese untie with white hair. After scanning all items.. I notice that I've forgotten my recycle back. And I requested for 2 plastic bag from her.. and just minus off the amount from my balance to return. She showed a unsatisfied body language as though that I've make her life miserable. In fact as I went to most supermarkets.. the cashier would prompt us on needing the plastic or not. But she didn't. I even apologise for any inconvenience cause. And it doesn't even change anything. I'm used to go there since my younger days..and nothing like this happens before... she shows such a rude behaviour to me and my daughters saw that and even commented on that. Well.. it might be convenient but it will be my last time going there.

Yesterday I went to buy honey and drink from this outlet. Was served by a woman named Maria. She was very kind to help me with the Merdeka discount claim as I didn't bring the cards. She helped me to locate the apps on my phone for the membership card to claim the points too. Keep it up with the good service.

Ms Maria is always very helpful at the self-check out counter. She has a warm smile and was very courteous — she helped to pack our items. Whenever we come to this branch, she always greets my family and I. Thumbs up to her customer service 👍🏻

Went regularly to this outlet. So far the staffs there were so nice. Today met a young Indian Customer service girl who was very impolite. Merely asking her to separate Medicinal products and Food items, she raised her voice saying I was rude (in Malay). Then she walked away and asked her colleague to take over the counter. This is a first very bad experience there. Already reported this to the Company for further action.

I had difficulty scanning the items at the self check out counter today, 27/9. I was helped twice by your personnel Mr Viswa to check the items. After that,  even though he was busy, he continues to eye me to ensure I'm ok. That's a great service. Thank you Viswa!

place_name: Anytime Fitness Hong Kah North CC
review_text: A well-maintained and spacious gym with a great range of equipment for both strength training and cardio. The place is kept clean, and the air-conditioning makes workouts comfortable even during hot days. The staff are friendly and approachable, and the overall environment feels safe and welcoming. Convenient location within the community centre, making it easy to drop by for a quick workout anytime. Overall, a great place for a workout — though the lighting could be improved to make the space feel brighter and more energizing.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Hong Kah North CC
review_text:
Devesh is great as a trainer. Some of my experiences with other trainers is them forcing their mindset on you but Devesh is very open minded and listens to your concerns before adjusting the session.

I’ve truly enjoyed having him as a trainer and will def recommend others to try him out.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Hong Kah North CC
review_text:
Friendly staff. Special note to the young Malay guy, really helpful and friendly.

The staff are professional & always greet you with a smile when u enter. Overall a cozy, neighbourhood gym with good vibes.

place_name: Anytime Fitness Hong Kah North CC
review_text: Very clean and well maintained gym, staff is friendly and helpful too. The gym does not have a weighing scale or water cooler (only located 1 floor below the gym) but considering the size of the gym I guess it’s fine…? The equipment in the gym is more than sufficient for a full body workout. Will visit here more often as the vibes here is top notch!

place_name: Anytime Fitness Hong Kah North CC
review_text:
I came to this gym for the first time and realised that the gym is so much more cleaner compared to those I have been to. The staffs were very helpful in helping me find the toilet and water cooler.

But the only downside is that there are no showering facilities and toilet and water cooler is at level 3.
Nice place
